_meta.purpose: Tarot Minor Arcana 56 张牌单源数据层（文章生产 + 未来 Tarot 工具复用）。完整 56 条：第一批 16 宫廷牌 + 4 Ace（20）；第二批 12 张数字牌（Pentacles 6 + 阴影 Swords 3 + 标杆 Twos 3）；第三批补全剩余 24 张数字牌（Wands 3-9 七张 + Cups 3-9 七张 + Swords 4-8 五张 + Pentacles 4/5/9 三张）= Minor 56 张完整。
_meta.framework_source: Minor-Arcana-牌义竞品研究.md（含字段设计 / 水晶映射 / 16 宫廷差异化重心表 / 40 数字牌 progression 表 / 2 个 json 样例）
_meta.batch_scope: Batch 1 (20): 16 court cards (Page/Knight/Queen/King x Wands/Cups/Swords/Pentacles) + 4 Aces (number=1 of each suit). Batch 2 (12): 6 Pentacles numbers (Two/Three/Six/Seven/Eight/Ten) + 3 Swords shadow cards (Three/Nine/Ten of Swords) + 3 benchmark Twos (Two of Wands / Two of Cups / Two of Swords).
_meta.batch_2_scope: 12 张数字牌。优先级遵循研究 §6.2：(1) Pentacles 数字牌 6 张——物质花色全系偏正面，与 Shop CTA 商业协同最强，6 张覆盖 2/3/6/7/8/10 五个 progression 节点（choice / 协作 / harmony / patience / mastery / legacy）；(2) Swords 阴影牌 3 张（Three/Nine/Ten）——塔罗最著名的 3 张阴影牌，逆位段守 M3 合规口径（shadow aspect / invitation to reflect，禁 curse/bad omen/evil/disease prediction），Health 黑名单（不医疗化、不承诺疗效）+ Finances 黑名单（不投资建议）；(3) 标杆 Twos 3 张——数字 2 = choice/partnership 起步，验证 progression 中段（Two of Pentacles=balance resources / Two of Cups=partnership/attraction / Two of Swords=stalemate/avoidance / Two of Wands=future planning），4 张 Two 全部落到花色独有意象，禁模板感。所有 12 条 card_type='number'，court_role/court_archetype/court_role_meaning/as_feelings/as_person 全为 null（数字牌非宫廷牌）；progression_meaning + progression_position 按 §3.3 表逐张差异化。
_meta.batch_3_scope: 26 张数字牌，补全 Minor 至 56 张完整（任务原文按 24 张描述差集示例，但精确差集 = 56 - 已有 32 = 24 数字牌缺口 + 校验发现 Ten of Wands / Ten of Cups 两张 completion 牌也未在已有 32 条内，故本批实际追加 26 张以满足四花色各 14 张的完整性红线）。精确差集（按花色数字牌 2-10 各 9 张计）：Wands 缺 3/4/5/6/7/8/9/10（8 张，Fire progression：扩展→庆祝→竞争→胜利→捍卫→迅速→坚韧→过载）；Cups 缺 3/4/5/6/7/8/9/10（8 张，Water progression：庆祝→冷漠→遗憾→怀旧→迷惑→转身→满足→家族圆满）；Swords 缺 4/5/6/7/8（5 张，Air progression：静修→冲突→渡过→策略→束缚）；Pentacles 缺 4/5/9（3 张，Earth progression：守财→贫乏→自足）。所有 26 条 card_type='number'，court_*/as_feelings/as_person 全 null。progression_meaning + progression_position 严格按研究 §3.3 表逐张差异化：同数字×不同花色落独有（如 Four of Wands=庆祝休战 ≠ Four of Cups=冷漠厌倦 ≠ Four of Swords=静修复原 ≠ Four of Pentacles=守财占有），禁中段模板感。每数字 archetype 各异（3=扩张/首批/摩擦分化 / 4=稳固休整 / 5=冲突失去 / 6=和谐胜利 / 7=评估策略 / 8=精进迁移 / 9=圆满前夜）。合规重点牌：Five of Swords（冲突/掠夺）+ Seven of Swords（策略/隐蔽）逆位用 shadow aspect / realignment 口径，禁 curse/bad omen，reasoning 落该牌独有；Five of Pentacles（匮乏/排斥）守 Finances 黑名单（不投资建议、不承诺脱贫），谈 money mindset / 资源觉察；Five of Cups（失落/遗憾）逆位用 healing/forgiveness 口径，不医疗化情绪。水晶按四花色元素映射（Wands=Fire→carnelian/citrine/sunstone/tiger-eye/garnet；Cups=Water→moonstone/aquamarine/blue-lace-agate/rhodochrosite/labradorite/rose-quartz；Swords=Air→amethyst/selenite/sodalite/lapis/fluorite/angelite；Pentacles=Earth→aventurine/red-jasper/hematite/pyrite/jade/moss-agate），5 角色差异化，slug 全带 -meaning 后缀对齐 390 库（本次 26 张全部复用前两批已验证的 26 颗水晶 slug，无新引入 slug——任务原文提到 garnet 但实际生产中未使用，全部 slug 已在前两批 crystal_slug_audit 中逐颗核对存在）。
_meta.content_basis: Rider-Waite 通识（画面+正逆位）+ Biddy/Labyrinthos 牌意速查 + Minor-Arcana 研究文档 §3.2 宫廷差异化重心表 / §3.3 数字 progression 表 + 390 水晶库 overview.Intentions/Element（实查）+ Crystal Coven Pentacles 水晶共识
_meta.crystal_slug_rule: 水晶 slug 严格对齐 390 库 crystal-attributes / *-meaning.json 文件的 slug 字段，统一带 -meaning 后缀（如 quartz-meaning / moonstone-meaning / lapis-meaning）。⚠️ 注意：(1) 全部带 -meaning 后缀，非短形式；(2) Lapis Lazuli 在 390 库真实 slug = lapis-meaning（不是 lapis-lazuli-meaning，390 库无 lapis-lazuli-meaning.json 文件）；(3) Clear Quartz slug = quartz-meaning（Major tarot-knowledge.json 误用短 slug 'quartz'，本数据层已修正为 quartz-meaning）。slug 正确性以 390 库文件为准，防死链。
_meta.crystal_slug_audit: 已逐颗核对：24 颗水晶的 slug 全部来自 390 库 *-meaning.json 实际 slug 字段（carnelian-meaning / citrine-meaning / sunstone-meaning / tiger-eye-meaning / moonstone-meaning / aquamarine-meaning / blue-lace-agate-meaning / amethyst-meaning / selenite-meaning / lapis-meaning / sodalite-meaning / fluorite-meaning / angelite-meaning / aventurine-meaning / red-jasper-meaning / hematite-meaning / pyrite-meaning / jade-meaning / rhodochrosite-meaning / rose-quartz-meaning / smoky-quartz-meaning / garnet-meaning / labradorite-meaning / quartz-meaning）。线上 HEAD 验证：carnelian/citrine/tiger-eye/aquamarine/amethyst/selenite/sodalite/fluorite/aventurine/red-jasper/pyrite/jade/rose-quartz/smoky-quartz/labradorite/quartz 的 /gemstone/{slug}/ 当前 200；sunstone/blue-lace-agate/angelite/hematite/rhodochrosite/garnet/moonstone/lapis 当前 404 = WP post status=future 未 publish（非 slug 错误，未来上线即 200），CTA 预验证脚本按 meaning→shop search→healing-jewelry 三级降级处理。

court_role_map.knight: the quest / action phase — pursuing the suit's theme in motion, idealistic, driving forward

_meta.next_batch_recommendation: 本批次验证字段方案：court_role/court_archetype/as_feelings/as_person/progression_meaning/love/career/finances/yes_no 全字段顺，36 数字牌批次可直接复用此 schema，仅需填 progression_meaning/progression_position（数字牌专属）+ 跳过 court_*（数字牌为 null）+ Ace 复用本批 4 张样例。建议 36 张分 3 批（每批 12 张），按研究 §6.2 优先级：第二批=Pentacles 数字 5 + 阴影牌 3 + 标杆；第三批=Wands/Cups 中段；第四批=补全。

cards.name: Page of Wands
cards.court_role_meaning: Page = the apprentice phase of Fire — the moment passion has just been lit but has not yet found its direction; enthusiasm outranks experience, and everything feels possible.
cards.upright_meaning: A youth stands alone in a barren landscape, holding a living wand upright while the salamander on his tunic bites its own tail — the eternal spark. Around him the desert is empty, because his quest is not yet about territory but about the fire itself: the discovery of what makes him come alive. The Page of Wands is the apprentice of Fire, the part of you that has caught a spark of passion but has not yet shaped it into a plan. Upright, this card speaks to enthusiasm that is genuinely alive — exploring, sampling, chasing the next thing that lights you up — without yet needing to commit to a single path.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the Page's restless spark scatters into aimlessness. Hype burns hot and burns out; projects get started and abandoned; the card becomes a confession that enthusiasm, without a container, just consumes itself. This is not a judgement — it is an invitation to notice which fire is actually yours to tend, and to give one spark the patience to grow.
cards.psychological_lens: novelty-seeking + openness to experience; the dopamine of possibility before commitment costs arrive
cards.eastern_anchor: In Chan/Zen practice, the Page of Wands echoes '初心' (beginner's mind, Shunryu Suzuki) — the empty, ready cup that holds 'I don't know' and is therefore alive to everything. Carnelian, historically carried in East Asian and Tibetan practice as a stone of courage and life-force ('the blood of the heroes' in old Tibetan lore), pairs with this freshly lit, beginner's fire.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a carnelian in your dominant hand; name one spark you have been chasing out of hype, and one spark that genuinely feels like yours. Choose where to give the next month of attention.
cards.as_feelings: If Page of Wands represents how someone feels about you: they feel a spark — excited, curious, lit up by the novelty of you — but the feeling is still restless and unformed, more 'I want to find out' than 'I am here to stay'.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Page of Wands is youthful in spirit (regardless of age), adventurous, charming when enthusiastic, prone to leaping before looking, and magnetic precisely because their fire is unguarded — but they may leave a trail of half-finished beginnings behind them.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Page of Wands heralds a fresh spark of attraction — often long-distance, online, or with someone 'new' to your world. The energy is electric but untested; enjoy the chemistry without betting the farm on it yet.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card signals a new creative impulse, an exploratory project, or an appetite to switch to something that lights you up. It is the green light to investigate, not yet the green light to commit.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Page can point to a new income idea or side project — genuine potential, but it will need follow-through. Beware of chasing every shiny opportunity at once.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love, the spark fades into hot-and-cold behaviour, ghosting after a fast start, or chasing the high of attraction without the work of intimacy.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career, scattered enthusiasm, abandoned projects, or all-talk-no-action energy. Pick one direction and stay with it past the honeymoon.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances, impulse spending on 'passion projects' that never ship, or chasing trends rather than building a base.

best_overall.name: Carnelian
best_overall.reason: Carnelian is the classic Fire-starter stone — its Element=Fire, Intentions (Courage / Motivation / Creativity) in the 390 library map directly onto the Page of Wands' freshly lit passion and the courage to chase it.

best_upright.name: Sunstone
best_upright.reason: Sunstone (Fire, Confidence / Joy / Warmth) amplifies the Page's enthusiasm and optimistic exploration — like sunlight on a fresh spark.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When the Page's fire scatters into burnout, Smoky Quartz (grounding + release) helps drain the hype and return attention to what actually matters.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; keeps the spark warm without forcing premature commitment.

best_daily_wear.name: Citrine
best_daily_wear.reason: Citrine (Fire, Abundance / Motivation) is a wearable daily reminder to keep one spark tended rather than chasing ten.

cards.related_reason: Page→Knight progression in Wands (apprentice to quester); Ace of Wands is the suit's spark-source; The Fool shares the leap-of-faith / beginner's-mind theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Crystals mapped from 390 lib Element=Fire consensus (Carnelian / Citrine / Sunstone) + court-role shadow needs (Smoky Quartz for scattered-fire reversal). Court Coven has no Wands crystals (Pentacles only).

cards.name: Knight of Wands
cards.court_role_meaning: Knight = the quest phase of Fire — passion has become motion; the figure charges forward on a rearing horse, idealistic, brave, and prone to outrunning his own logistics.
cards.upright_meaning: The knight's horse rears beneath him; his tunic is patterned with salamanders; he charges forward with wand raised, having clearly already committed to the pursuit before the picture began. Where the Page discovers the spark, the Knight becomes it — a body in energetic motion toward what he wants. Upright, the Knight of Wands is momentum with a heartbeat: brave, alive, willing to risk the leap, and capable of carrying others with the heat of his conviction.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same momentum curdles into recklessness — charging without a target, fury without an aim, leaving bridges and unfinished business behind. The shadow is not the speed itself; it is speed without a map. This card invites you to ask what you are actually riding toward before you spur the horse again.
cards.psychological_lens: approach motivation + high arousal; the energized pursuit system before impulse control catches up
cards.eastern_anchor: The Knight of Wands resonates with the Tibetan dharmapāla (护法) energy — fierce, fast, protective drive that must be yoked to a clear vow or it becomes destructive. Tiger's Eye, used across Han and Tibetan traditions as a protective warrior's stone, pairs with this brave, fast-moving force that needs a true north.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a tiger-eye stone; name the destination you are charging toward in one sentence. If you cannot name it, pause before the next spur of action.
cards.as_feelings: If Knight of Wands represents how someone feels about you: they feel lit up and on the move toward you — passionate, sexually charged, decisive — but the feeling can run hot and may cool fast if the chase ends.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Knight of Wands is bold, charismatic, sexually magnetic, a doer not a deliberator; inspiring to be around and exhausting to keep up with. He keeps his promises when he remembers them, and forgets the ones that no longer thrill him.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Knight of Wands is the lover who arrives fast and hot — chemistry, pursuit, adventure, possibly long-distance or short-fuse. Enjoy the ride; do not mistake the chase for the foundation.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card is the entrepreneurial push, the bold move, the launch, the pitch. Excellent for momentum, risky if strategy is missing.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, an appetite for bold bets; potential for fast gains and fast losses. Pair the impulse with a budget.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: hot temper, jealousy, impulsive decisions you regret, or someone who burns through relationships.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: reckless risk, alienated teammates, abandoned launches, charging into walls.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: gambling energy, chasing losses, sunk-cost spirals.

best_overall.name: Carnelian
best_overall.reason: Carnelian's Fire + Courage/Motivation/Action Intentions match the Knight's action-phase energy — the engine of the charge itself.

best_upright.name: Tiger's Eye
best_upright.reason: Tiger's Eye (Courage / Confidence / Protection) gives the Knight's bravery a protective backbone — courage with eyes open.

best_reversed.name: Hematite
best_reversed.reason: When the Knight's momentum becomes recklessness, Hematite (grounding + strength) pulls the rider back onto the earth and into the body.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; softens the chase into warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Sunstone
best_daily_wear.reason: Sunstone (Fire, Joy / Confidence) worn daily keeps the adventurous warmth alive without tipping into burnout.

cards.related_reason: Page→Knight→Queen progression in Wands; Ace of Wands is the suit's source; The Chariot (Major 7) shares the driven forward momentum.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Element=Fire consensus (Carnelian / Tiger's Eye / Sunstone); Hematite added for reversal grounding.

cards.name: Queen of Wands
cards.court_role_meaning: Queen = the mastery-and-containment phase of Fire — passion has been internalized into a steady, radiant presence; she sits throneed with the black cat of independence at her feet and holds both wand and sunflower, magnetic without performing.
cards.upright_meaning: The Queen of Wands sits on her throne with a sunflower in one hand (turning toward the sun) and the wand of her suit in the other, the black cat of self-possession and quiet power curled at her feet. Unlike the Knight who charges, the Queen magnetizes — she does not chase, she attracts. Upright, this card is the part of you that has done the work of internalizing Fire: confidence that is no longer performed but radiated, leadership that pulls rather than pushes, a presence that fills a room by being fully itself.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the magnetic field collapses into need — the same charm becomes a performance for validation, the warmth turns into a competition for the spotlight, and underneath it sits the insecurity that no amount of applause has been able to fix. The card is not a verdict; it is an invitation to find the source of the leak rather than pouring more fire into a sieve.
cards.psychological_lens: core confidence (self-efficacy, not narcissism); the difference between secure and insecure charisma
cards.eastern_anchor: The Queen of Wands resonates with the Tibetan image of Tārā (度母) — the radiant, fearless feminine who acts with warmth and without apology. Sunstone, with its sun-turning optical effect (aventurescence) historically called 'sun-sparkle' across Asian traditions, mirrors her sunflower and her turn toward the source of warmth.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a piece of sunstone at your solar plexus; ask what you have been performing for applause that you could simply allow to be yours by being present.
cards.as_feelings: If Queen of Wands represents how someone feels about you: they feel a warm, confident, chosen-by-you attraction — you light them up by being fully yourself, not by flattering them. The magnetism is mutual.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Queen of Wands is self-possessed, warm, charismatic, often creative or entrepreneurial, comfortable being seen, fiercely loyal to those she loves, and allergic to smallness. She does not shrink to make others comfortable — and she can be impatient with people who do.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Queen of Wands signals confident, warm attraction — the version of you (or a partner) who knows their own worth and loves generously without losing themselves. A good omen for mutual chemistry.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card marks a leadership moment — being seen, taking the stage, leading from presence rather than title. Visibility favours you now.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Queen of Wands supports confident financial presence — asking for the rate, raising the price, owning the value. Pair with clear accounting so the confidence is grounded.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: jealousy, performative affection, or seeking validation outside the relationship. The insecurity underneath needs attention, not more attention.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: office politics, competitiveness, burnout from chasing visibility, or fear of being seen that masks as arrogance.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: spending to be seen, lifestyle inflation tied to image, or undercharging out of self-doubt.

best_overall.name: Sunstone
best_overall.reason: Sunstone (Fire, Confidence / Joy / Warmth) is the perfect mirror of the Queen's sunflower — radiant self-possession turned toward the source of warmth, leadership that attracts rather than performs.

best_upright.name: Carnelian
best_upright.reason: Carnelian (Fire, Courage / Creativity) amplifies her confident, creative vitality.

best_reversed.name: Rose Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When the Queen's magnetism collapses into need for validation, Rose Quartz (Heart, self-love) gently rebuilds the inner worth that applause cannot fix.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: House-standard love intention; the Queen who loves without losing herself.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger's Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger's Eye (Courage / Confidence / Protection) worn daily supports steady self-possession and protects against energy drain from others.

cards.related_reason: Queen→King progression in Wands; Page of Wands is her apprentice echo; Ace of Wands is the suit's source; Strength (Major 8) shares the tamed-fire, inner-mastery theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Sunstone chosen best_overall for the direct sunflower/sun-sparkle symbolic match + Element=Fire; research §5.2 table.

cards.name: King of Wands
cards.court_role_meaning: King = the mastery-and-mastery phase of Fire — passion has become vision and structure; he sits throneed with salamanders, holding the wand as a scepter of direction, the leader who points at a horizon others had not yet seen.
cards.upright_meaning: The King of Wands sits on his throne adorned with salamanders and lions, the wand of his suit held as a scepter, his gaze fixed on a horizon beyond the frame. Where the Knight charges and the Queen magnetizes, the King builds — he takes Fire and turns it into a direction others can follow, a venture others can join, a future others had not yet imagined. Upright, this card is visionary leadership grounded in the courage to act: the founder, the director, the one who sees the whole map and is willing to commit to a path on it.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same authority hardens into domination — vision without execution, charisma weaponized, impatience with anyone slower, the leader who confuses fear with respect. The shadow is not the vision itself; it is the refusal to share it. This card invites the King to ask whose strength, besides his own, his kingdom is actually built on.
cards.psychological_lens: transformational leadership; vision + agency + the discipline to translate long-term aim into coordinated action
cards.eastern_anchor: The King of Wands resonates with the Tibetan image of the dharmarāja (法王) — the visionary spiritual king whose authority comes from clarity of vision and service to the path, not from force. Tiger's Eye, traditionally carried as a stone of the commander who protects what matters, pairs with this vision-anchored leadership.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a tiger-eye stone; speak aloud the one vision you are willing to commit the next year of your life to — and name one person whose strength you will need to make it real.
cards.as_feelings: If King of Wands represents how someone feels about you: they feel clear, decided, and committed — not the Page's restless spark or the Knight's hot chase, but a settled 'I have chosen you' that holds steady under pressure.
cards.as_person: As a person, the King of Wands is a natural leader, decisive, far-seeing, charismatic, often entrepreneurial; he inspires loyalty when his vision includes others and resistance when it does not. He is impatient with smallness and can mistake his own pace for the team's.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the King of Wands signals a settled, protective, decisive partner — or the version of you who shows up clear about what (and whom) you want. Chemistry with a foundation.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card marks the founder/leader moment — taking the helm, setting direction, building the venture, owning the long-term vision. Excellent for founding, executive, and entrepreneurial moves.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the King of Wands supports long-horizon wealth-building, leadership in business ownership, and bold but considered moves. Vision with execution.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: controlling partner, impatience, the relationship as an extension of his image rather than a shared life.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: the boss who rules by fear, the founder who cannot delegate, the vision that never ships because details are 'beneath' him.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: over-leveraged bets, money tied to ego, risky leadership decisions that put others' stakes at risk.

best_overall.name: Tiger's Eye
best_overall.reason: Tiger's Eye (Courage / Confidence / Protection / Clarity of intent) is the perfect stone for the King's vision-anchored leadership — the commander who sees clearly and protects what he has built.
best_overall.source: 390 lib Element=Fire + Intentions; Crystal Coven King-of-Pentacles consensus (cross-court commander stone)

best_upright.reason: Citrine (Fire, Abundance / Confidence / Motivation) amplifies the King's abundance-mindset and the will to build.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When authority becomes domination, Smoky Quartz (grounding + release of ego-heat) cools the tyrant and returns the King to service of the vision rather than his own image.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; keeps the King's protectiveness warm rather than controlling.

best_daily_wear.name: Carnelian
best_daily_wear.reason: Carnelian worn daily supports the steady courage and life-force a long-term vision requires.

cards.name: Page of Cups
cards.suit_theme: emotion / intuition / relationship / creativity of the heart
cards.court_role_meaning: Page = the apprentice phase of Water — the heart's beginner; the figure in blue stands at the seashore as a fish emerges from his cup, a message from the unconscious he is just learning to listen to.
cards.upright_meaning: A youth in a floral blue tunic stands at the water's edge holding a golden cup; from the cup a small fish emerges, like a message from a fairytale or a dream surfacing into waking life. The Page of Cups is the apprentice of Water — the part of you just beginning to listen to feeling, to take inner promptings seriously, to receive the unexpected messenger that the unconscious sends. Upright, this card speaks to a tender new emotional or creative beginning, the first whisper of an intuition asking to be heard.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the Page's dreaminess curdles into avoidance — sensitivity becomes withdrawal, the inner child acts out instead of plays, daydreaming replaces doing, and the cup is used to hide rather than to hold. This is not a judgement on tenderness; it is an invitation to give the feeling a small, real-world container so it can grow up rather than flood.
cards.psychological_lens: openness to experience + the beginner's mind in emotional life; the capacity to receive affective signals before the intellect edits them
cards.eastern_anchor: The fish emerging from the cup echoes the Eastern image of the lotus rising from muddy water (出淤泥而不染) — something emerging from the depths of the unconscious into form. Moonstone, sacred in both Indian and East Asian traditions as a stone of the moon's receptive, reflective principle (Chandra / Soma), pairs naturally with this watery, intuitive page.
cards.recommended_practice: Before sleep, hold a moonstone; ask your unconscious one question you have been avoiding. Record whatever surfaces in the morning, without editing.
cards.as_feelings: If Page of Cups represents how someone feels about you: they feel a tender, curious, slightly awkward budding attraction — a crush in its earliest stirrings, more poetry than plan, easily moved and easily bruised.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Page of Cups is youthful in spirit (in age or in heart), imaginative, sensitive, often artistically gifted but emotionally unpracticed; they feel before they understand, and they need a safe container to grow into the feeling.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Page of Cups heralds a tender new beginning — a message, a confession, a small romantic gesture, an 'I like you' that took courage to say. If single, someone may approach with gentle, slightly nervous interest.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card points to creative fields (art, music, writing, fashion, film) or a fresh creative impulse entering your current work. Watch for daydreaming without follow-through.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Page can signal romantic financial daydreams (the lottery fantasy, the windfall hope); ground goals in achievable steps rather than waiting for magic.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: emotional immaturity, shyness mistaken for disinterest, insecurity blocking connection, or escaping into fantasy rather than showing up.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: creative blocks, mood interfering with professionalism, or all-ideas-no-execution energy.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: avoidance of money reality, magical thinking about income, denial about debts.

best_overall.name: Moonstone
best_overall.reason: Moonstone's Water element + Intuition / New Beginnings / Calm Intentions in the 390 library map directly onto the Page's watery, dreamy, intuitive-messenger archetype.

best_upright.name: Aquamarine
best_upright.reason: Aquamarine (Water, Courage + Communication + Calm) amplifies the Page's message-bearing quality — the courage to voice a tender feeling.

best_reversed.name: Rhodochrosite
best_reversed.reason: When the Page's sensitivity reverses into emotional immaturity, Rhodochrosite (self-love + inner-child healing) gently matures the wounded child rather than indulging it.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention.

best_daily_wear.name: Blue Lace Agate
best_daily_wear.reason: Blue Lace Agate (Water, Calm + Communication + Soothing) is a wearable daily reminder of gentle, honest emotional expression.

cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Labyrinthos keywords (upright 7 + reversed 7) used as keyword source. Crystals mapped from Water-suit consensus (Moonstone / Aquamarine / Blue Lace Agate all Element=Water in 390 lib); Rhodochrosite for reversal inner-child healing. Research §4.3 verified sample.

cards.name: Knight of Cups
cards.suit_theme: emotion / intuition / relationship / creativity of the heart
cards.court_role_meaning: Knight = the quest phase of Water — feeling has become pursuit; the knight rides a white horse at an unhurried pace, holding aloft a cup as if offering a gift or a proposal, chasing beauty rather than conquest.
cards.upright_meaning: The Knight of Cups rides a slow white horse, his cup raised like an offering or a proposal — unlike the Knight of Wands who charges, this knight moves at the pace of feeling, arrives with grace, and offers something rather than taking it. Upright, this card is the part of you (or someone coming toward you) that is pursuing beauty, love, or an ideal — a romantic gesture, a creative offering, an invitation extended in good faith. The quest is for meaning, not victory.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same idealism curdles — fantasy replaces follow-through, charm without substance, moodiness and disappointment when reality does not match the dream. The shadow is not the romance itself; it is the refusal to love the real person instead of the idea of them. The card invites you to ground the dream in a relationship, a project, or a self that actually exists.
cards.psychological_lens: affective idealization + the pursuit of meaning; the gap between projected fantasy and the real beloved
cards.eastern_anchor: The Knight of Cups resonates with the East Asian ideal of the '雅' (yǎ, refined elegance) — the cultivated pursuit of beauty, poetry, and right feeling as a way of being. Aquamarine, traditionally the sailor's talisman for safe passage across water, pairs with this knight's gentle pursuit across the realm of feeling.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an aquamarine at the throat; speak one beautiful, true thing you have been idealizing — and name what it would take to bring it into real form this month.
cards.as_feelings: If Knight of Cups represents how someone feels about you: they feel a tender, romantic, slightly idealized attraction — they are pursuing the beauty of you, possibly more the idea than the reality. Enjoy the courtship; gently invite them to know the real you too.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Knight of Cups is charming, artistic, emotionally expressive, idealistic, sometimes moody; he woos with words and gestures, means them in the moment, and can be slow to follow through when the romance meets the mundane.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Knight of Cups is the classic suitor — a proposal, a confession, a romantic gesture, an invitation. Often an actual message arriving. Real chemistry, possibly slightly idealized at the start.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card signals pursuing work you actually love — a creative offer, a meaningful project, an invitation to bring more artistry into what you do. Just make sure the proposal has legs.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Knight can point to money arriving through charm, artistry, or a creative offer — and warns against spending on image and romance beyond your means.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: moodiness, broken promises, fantasy partner who disappears when the dream meets reality, empty flattery.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: all-vision-no-execution, projects pursued for glamour not substance, burnout when the dream loses its shine.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: spending to impress, romanticizing investments, mood-driven money decisions.

best_overall.name: Aquamarine
best_overall.reason: Aquamarine (Water, Courage / Communication / Calm) is the knight's gentle-pursuit stone — the courage to voice feeling and ride toward beauty without force.

best_upright.name: Rose Quartz
best_upright.reason: Rose Quartz (Heart, unconditional love) amplifies the Knight's romantic warmth and the sincerity of the offering.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When the romantic dream curdles into escapism, Smoky Quartz (grounding + release) returns the dreamer to the real world and the real relationship.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention.

best_daily_wear.name: Moonstone
best_daily_wear.reason: Moonstone worn daily keeps the intuitive, receptive quality of the heart alive without losing touch with reality.

cards.related_reason: Page→Knight→Queen progression in Cups; Ace of Cups is the suit's source; The Lovers (Major 6) shares the romantic-choice theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Aquamarine chosen best_overall per research §5.2 (water + pursuit + communication).

cards.name: Queen of Cups
cards.suit_theme: emotion / intuition / relationship / creativity of the heart
cards.court_role_meaning: Queen = the mastery-and-containment phase of Water — feeling has been internalized into a steady, receptive compassion; she sits at the water's edge holding an ornate closed cup, the only court figure whose cup is closed — she holds emotion without being flooded by it.
cards.upright_meaning: The Queen of Cups sits on her throne at the edge of the sea, her ornate cup closed and held carefully so that nothing spills — a deliberate image of holding deep feeling without being flooded by it. Unlike the Knight who pursues, the Queen receives; she is the safe harbour others come to, the part of you that has learned to feel deeply and stay steady. Upright, this card is mature compassion, emotional intelligence, the capacity to sit with another's pain without drowning in it.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the closed cup overflows — empathy becomes merger, the Queen gives until she is empty, takes on feelings that were never hers to carry, and mistakes self-abandonment for love. The shadow is not the compassion itself; it is the missing boundary that lets it leak out. The card invites you to ask whose emotion you are holding that was always meant to stay with them.
cards.psychological_lens: empathic concern vs emotional contagion; the difference between compassionate presence and enmeshment
cards.eastern_anchor: The Queen of Cups resonates with the figure of Guanyin (观世音) — the East Asian bodhisattva of compassion who hears the cries of the world and responds without being overwhelmed by them. Rose Quartz, the house stone of the heart across both Western and Eastern traditions, mirrors her closed-yet-full cup of steady compassion.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a rose-quartz at the heart; imagine the cup of your care for one specific person. Set it down. Notice whose emotion remains with you that was never yours.
cards.as_feelings: If Queen of Cups represents how someone feels about you: they feel safe, seen, and tenderly held by you — a love that does not require you to perform, and that stays steady even when you are not at your best.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Queen of Cups is warm, emotionally intelligent, a deep listener, a healer or caretaker by nature, often artistic; she holds space beautifully and risks holding too much. Her growth edge is the boundary that lets her give without disappearing.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Queen of Cups signals deep, steady, emotionally intelligent love — a partner (or the version of you) who can hold the full range of feeling without flinching. Real intimacy, not just chemistry.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours helping, healing, counselling, HR, diplomacy, and any role that calls for emotional intelligence and a steady presence under pressure.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Queen supports generous, heart-aligned money decisions — and warns against over-giving, lending what you cannot afford to lose, or undercharging out of misplaced empathy.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: co-dependency, emotional merger, martyrdom, or staying in a relationship by disappearing into the other person.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: burnout from carrying others' loads, taking on colleagues' stress, lacking boundaries with clients.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: lending to people who will not return it, giving services away, financial self-erasure.

best_overall.name: Rose Quartz
best_overall.reason: Rose Quartz (Heart, unconditional love / compassion) mirrors the Queen's closed-yet-full cup — deep feeling held with steady, boundaried warmth.

best_upright.name: Moonstone
best_upright.reason: Moonstone (Water, Intuition / Calm) deepens the Queen's receptive, intuitive quality.

best_reversed.name: Labradorite
best_reversed.reason: When the Queen merges with others' emotions, Labradorite (Water, Protection + Transformation) shields the aura and restores the boundary between self and other.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; the Queen who loves without disappearing.

best_daily_wear.name: Aquamarine
best_daily_wear.reason: Aquamarine worn daily supports calm, clear emotional communication — receiving and expressing feeling without flooding.

cards.related_reason: Queen→King progression in Cups; Page of Cups is her apprentice echo; Ace of Cups is the suit's source; The Empress (Major 3) shares the nurturing, abundant feminine theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Rose Quartz chosen best_overall per research §5.2 (compassion + heart queen). Labradorite for reversal boundary.

cards.name: King of Cups
cards.suit_theme: emotion / intuition / relationship / creativity of the heart
cards.court_role_meaning: King = the mastery-and-mastery phase of Water — emotion has been mastered into a vessel that holds without spilling; he sits on a throne set in choppy water, the ship behind him riding a wave while he stays steady — feeling without being ruled by feeling.
cards.upright_meaning: The King of Cups sits on a throne set directly in the sea; behind him a ship rides a wave, a dolphin leaps, and yet his cup remains level and his posture calm — he is the only figure in the deck who has fully mastered the element his suit rules. Unlike the Queen who receives, the King holds — feeling has become a steady vessel he can offer from without being emptied or flooded by. Upright, this card is mature emotional mastery: the calm in the storm, the diplomat, the counsellor, the elder who has been through the waves and no longer drowns in them.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same mastery curdles into manipulation — feelings used as leverage, the silent treatment as a weapon, generosity weaponized into guilt, calmness weaponized into coldness. The shadow is not the mastery itself; it is mastery turned to control. The card invites the King to ask whether his calm is wisdom or a way to keep others off-balance.
cards.psychological_lens: emotional regulation at the highest tier; the integration of affective depth with steady executive function
cards.eastern_anchor: The King of Cups resonates with the Tibetan image of the 'mind like a clear lake' — agitated by no wave, reflecting everything accurately. Lapis Lazuli, sacred across Tibetan, Persian, and Egyptian traditions as the stone of inner vision and royal wisdom, pairs with this mastery of the inner water through clear seeing.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a lapis lazuli at the third eye or throat; name one feeling you have been holding behind a calm mask. Allow yourself to feel it fully for one minute before deciding whether to speak it.
cards.as_feelings: If King of Cups represents how someone feels about you: they feel a steady, mature, balanced love — emotional without being dramatic, present without being flooded, the kind of feeling you can build a life on.
cards.as_person: As a person, the King of Cups is emotionally mature, calm under pressure, generous, wise, often a counsellor or elder figure; he holds feeling without leaking it and helps others do the same. His shadow is using that calm to control rather than to serve.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the King of Cups signals a mature, balanced partner — or the version of you who can hold the full range of a relationship's feeling without being destabilized by it. Real, durable love.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours counselling, diplomacy, leadership roles requiring emotional steadiness, and any position where calm under pressure is the job.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the King supports balanced, generous, wise money stewardship — giving without self-erasure, holding wealth without being held by it.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: emotional manipulation, cold shoulder, guilt-as-weapon, calm used to keep a partner destabilized.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: passive-aggressive leadership, withholding information, mood-driven decisions dressed up as wisdom.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: manipulative generosity, money used as leverage, guilt-tied gifts.

best_overall.name: Lapis Lazuli
best_overall.reason: Lapis Lazuli (Air-but-governs-the-upper-water centres, Wisdom / Truth / Inner Vision) is the king's mastery-of-feeling-through-clear-seeing stone — the calm that comes from seeing accurately, not from suppressing.

best_upright.name: Aquamarine
best_upright.reason: Aquamarine (Water, Calm / Communication) deepens the King's calm communication of feeling.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When mastery curdles into manipulation, Smoky Quartz (grounding + release) drains the cold shadow and returns the King to honest feeling.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention.

best_daily_wear.name: Moonstone
best_daily_wear.reason: Moonstone worn daily keeps the receptive, lunar quality of the heart alive alongside mastery.

cards.related_reason: King←Queen←Knight progression in Cups; Ace of Cups is the suit's source; The Hierophant (Major 5) shares the wisdom-elder / mature-authority theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Lapis chosen best_overall per research §5.2 (wisdom + king of feeling through clear seeing). ⚠️ 390 lib slug = lapis-meaning (file lapis-meaning.json), NOT lapis-lazuli-meaning.

cards.name: Page of Swords
cards.court_role_meaning: Page = the apprentice phase of Air — the mind's beginner; the youth stands on a windy height, sword raised, hair blown back, the birds of thought circling overhead, alert and curious and ready to defend a position he has not yet tested.
cards.upright_meaning: A youth stands on a raised mound in a windy landscape, sword held upright and both hands on the hilt, hair blown back by the air of his own element, birds wheeling overhead — alert, curious, mentally alive, and a little defensive. The Page of Swords is the apprentice of Air, the part of you that has just discovered the power of the question, the sharp observation, the willingness to look harder. Upright, this card speaks to intellectual curiosity, mental agility, the courage to ask the question everyone else is avoiding, and the vigilance of a mind freshly awake.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same sharpness becomes a weapon without a target — gossip, hasty judgement, paranoia, talking before thinking, defending positions you have not examined. The shadow is not the curiosity itself; it is curiosity without honesty about its own bias. The card invites you to ask what you actually want to know versus what you want to be right about.
cards.psychological_lens: intellectual openness + the vigilance system; the difference between inquiry and interrogation
cards.eastern_anchor: The Page of Swords resonates with the Chan Buddhist ideal of '疑情' (yíqíng, the kernel of doubt that fuels genuine inquiry) — the question that does not settle for an easy answer. Sodalite, traditionally a stone of logic and honest self-inquiry, pairs with this page's commitment to look harder, including at himself.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a sodalite at the throat or third eye; ask one question you have been defending an answer to. Sit with the question for one minute before allowing any answer to land.
cards.as_feelings: If Page of Swords represents how someone feels about you: they feel curious, mentally engaged, a little guarded — intrigued enough to keep watching you, not yet committed enough to drop their defences.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Page of Swords is clever, articulate, observant, mentally restless, often young or newly into an intellectual phase; they ask sharp questions and sometimes use them to score points. Their growth edge is curiosity in service of truth rather than performance.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Page of Swords signals curiosity and mental chemistry, possibly guarded attraction, a flirtation built on banter. Real interest, but watch for someone keeping their defences up longer than is fair to you.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours research, writing, journalism, study, and any pursuit requiring sharp observation and a willingness to ask hard questions.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Page supports research before financial decisions — read the contract, ask the questions, watch the small print.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: gossip, suspicion, paranoia, snark used as flirtation, second-guessing the relationship out of habit.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: gossip at work, talking without doing, hasty conclusions, leaking information.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: suspicious of legitimate opportunities, signing without reading, or schemes that look too clever.

best_overall.name: Sodalite
best_overall.reason: Sodalite (Air, Clarity / Logic / Honesty) maps onto the Page's apprentice-of-the-mind archetype — curiosity disciplined by honest inquiry rather than performance.

best_upright.name: Fluorite
best_upright.reason: Fluorite (Air, Focus / Clarity / Decision) sharpens the Page's mental focus and helps separate signal from noise.

best_reversed.name: Amethyst
best_reversed.reason: When the Page's sharpness becomes gossip or paranoia, Amethyst (Air, Calm / Spiritual awareness) cools the over-active mind and returns it to honest seeing.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; softens the Page's guarded edge.

best_daily_wear.name: Angelite
best_daily_wear.reason: Angelite (Air, Calm / Gentle communication) worn daily supports gentle, honest expression rather than sharp retort.

cards.related_reason: Page→Knight progression in Swords; Ace of Swords is the suit's source; Justice (Major 11) shares the truth-seeking theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Sodalite chosen best_overall per research §5.2 (logic + curious mind).

cards.name: Knight of Swords
cards.court_role_meaning: Knight = the quest phase of Air — the mind in hot pursuit of truth; the knight charges at full gallop, sword lowered like a lance, horse's hooves off the ground, both trees and birds bending before him — pure directed mental force in motion.
cards.upright_meaning: The Knight of Swords charges at full gallop, his sword thrust forward like a lance, his horse's hooves lifted clear off the ground, trees and birds bending aside in the wind of his passage. Where the Knight of Wands charges with passion, this knight charges with conviction — a directed mental force in pursuit of truth, a goal, a verdict. Upright, this card is relentless, focused, fast; the courage to name what is true and the will to act on it without flinching.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same directed force becomes recklessness — charging without checking, talking without listening, winning the argument and losing the relationship, no brakes on a fast-moving mind. The shadow is not the directness itself; it is directness without care for the cost. The card invites the Knight to ask what his speed is protecting him from feeling.
cards.psychological_lens: goal-directed drive + verbal directness at high activation; the cost of relentless approach without reflection
cards.eastern_anchor: The Knight of Swords resonates with the Tibetan image of Manjushri's sword (文殊菩萨慧剑) — the blade of discriminating wisdom that cuts through delusion in a single stroke. Selenite, a stone of mental clarity and cleansing named for selēnē (moon) in Greek and used for clearing the mind across contemplative traditions, pairs with this clean, fast, truth-cutting force.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a selenite wand; name one truth you have been charging toward stating. Ask whether stating it now, in this way, serves the truth or only your momentum.
cards.as_feelings: If Knight of Swords represents how someone feels about you: they feel decided, direct, possibly intense — they have made up their mind about you and may be moving fast to act on it. The feeling is clear, not always gentle.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Knight of Swords is brilliant, direct, ambitious, articulate, fast-thinking, impatient with slowness or dishonesty; he wins arguments and can leave casualties behind him. His growth edge is recognizing that being right and being kind are not always opposed.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Knight of Swords is direct attraction pursued fast — a clear 'yes', a quick escalation, possibly an intensity that needs tempering. Not subtle, often honest, occasionally blunt.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours aggressive pursuit of a goal — pitches, campaigns, fast decisive moves, debate, advocacy. Excellent for momentum, risky on relationships.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Knight supports fast, decisive financial action — and warns against impulsive trades or winning a deal at a cost you cannot afford.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: aggression, impatience, arguments won at the cost of the relationship, intensity without warmth.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: ruthlessness, burned bridges, allies alienated, momentum that crashes into walls.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: impulsive trades, over-leverage, schemes that prioritize speed over diligence.

best_overall.name: Selenite
best_overall.reason: Selenite (Air, Clarity / Cleansing / Calm) is the knight's truth-cutting stone — the clean blade of discriminating wisdom that sees clearly and acts without distortion.

best_upright.name: Amethyst
best_upright.reason: Amethyst (Air, Calm / Spiritual awareness) keeps the Knight's intensity from tipping into aggression — clarity with composure.

best_reversed.name: Sodalite
best_reversed.reason: When speed becomes recklessness, Sodalite (Logic / Honesty) slows the Knight enough to examine his own assumptions.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention.

best_daily_wear.name: Lapis Lazuli
best_daily_wear.reason: Lapis worn daily supports wisdom alongside directness — truth tempered by understanding.

cards.related_reason: Page→Knight→Queen progression in Swords; Ace of Swords is the suit's source; Justice (Major 11) shares the truth-verdict theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Selenite chosen best_overall per research §5.2 (clarity + truth knight).

cards.name: Queen of Swords
cards.court_role_meaning: Queen = the mastery-and-containment phase of Air — the mind has been internalized into independent clarity; she sits throneed, sword upright before her, hand raised in a gesture that says 'I see through this', her child on the throne beside her nodding toward the truth she will not flinch from.
reversed_keywords: carrying old wounds into new situations
cards.upright_meaning: The Queen of Swords sits upright on her throne, her sword held vertically before her like the line between truth and comfort, her hand raised in the gesture of one who has earned the right to say 'I see exactly what is happening here'. She is the only figure in the deck whose clarity is fully internalized — she does not need consensus to know what she sees. Upright, this card is independent discernment, honest communication, firm boundaries, the willingness to name the hard truth kindly and without apology.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same clarity freezes — the Queen becomes cold, bitter, weaponizes her perceptiveness, isolates in the name of independence, and drags wounds from old betrayals into situations that do not deserve them. The shadow is not the discernment itself; it is discernment hardened against connection. The card invites the Queen to ask what she is protecting with the coldness, and whether the wall is still serving her or has become her prison.
cards.psychological_lens: differentiation of self + cognitive clarity; the difference between healthy independence and defended isolation
cards.eastern_anchor: The Queen of Swords resonates with the East Asian image of Prajñāpāramitā (般若波罗蜜多) — the feminine perfection of wisdom that sees things exactly as they are, without distortion. Lapis Lazuli, the stone of inner vision and the perceptive wisdom of the third eye across Tibetan and Persian traditions, pairs with her clear, sovereign seeing.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a lapis lazuli at the third eye; name one truth you have been softening to keep others comfortable. Decide whether now is the moment to state it plainly.
cards.as_feelings: If Queen of Swords represents how someone feels about you: they feel clear-eyed and a little guarded — they see you accurately, including the parts you have not shown them yet. The feeling is honest, not always warm, but real.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Queen of Swords is intelligent, independent, direct, perceptive, often single or widowed in traditional readings (she has known loss and learned from it), uncomfortable with dishonesty, allergic to manipulation. She sets boundaries cleanly and can cut when they are crossed.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Queen of Swords signals a partner (or the version of you) who values honesty and independence over romance — clear communication, real boundaries, no games. An excellent card for mature, sober partnership.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours law, analysis, counselling, editing, leadership roles requiring discernment, and any position where naming the truth is the job.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Queen supports clear-eyed money decisions — read contracts, set terms, do not be charmed out of your discernment.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: coldness, withholding, dragging old betrayals into a new relationship, weaponizing perceptiveness.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: harsh, isolating leadership, perfectionism weaponized, allies pushed away.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: cynical about legitimate offers, contracts weaponized, punishing terms.

best_upright.reason: Sodalite (Logic / Honesty) deepens the Queen's honest, logical assessment of any situation.

best_reversed.reason: When clarity hardens into coldness, Rose Quartz (Heart, compassion) softens the Queen's wall without weakening her discernment.

cards.name: King of Swords
cards.court_role_meaning: King = the mastery-and-mastery phase of Air — the mind has become sovereign authority; he sits throneed with sword upright in his right hand (action) and his left hand in a gesture of receipt, the butterfly of transformed thought on his throne — truth that has been earned, structured, and offered outward as law, ethics, or final word.
cards.upright_meaning: The King of Swords sits on his throne with his sword raised upright in the hand of action, his other hand open in receiving, the butterfly of transformed mind carved into his throne — the only figure in the deck whose mastery of Air has become structure, ethics, and authority others are bound by. Where the Queen discerns, the King decides. Upright, this card is the principled authority — clear judgement grounded in ethics, the willingness to deliver the fair verdict, intellectual leadership that does not bend to convenience.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same authority becomes tyranny — rules weaponized, intellect used to dominate, principle dressed up to excuse cruelty, the final word delivered without listening to the case. The shadow is not the authority itself; it is authority without humility. The card invites the King to ask whose voice he has stopped hearing because he decided long ago he did not need to.
cards.psychological_lens: principled authority + ethical leadership; the difference between earned authority and defended dogma
cards.eastern_anchor: The King of Swords resonates with the Tibetan dharmarāja of clear perception — the king whose sovereignty rests on seeing accurately and judging ethically. Lapis Lazuli, the stone of sovereign wisdom in Tibetan, Persian, and Egyptian royal traditions, pairs with this principled, truth-grounded authority.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a lapis lazuli at the throat; name one verdict you have been preparing to deliver. Ask whether your judgement is grounded in present evidence or in an old certainty you have stopped examining.
cards.as_feelings: If King of Swords represents how someone feels about you: they feel decided, principled, and steady — not the Queen's discerning reserve but a settled clarity about where they stand with you. The feeling is reliable, occasionally austere.
cards.as_person: As a person, the King of Swords is principled, intelligent, fair, often in a position of authority (legal, medical, ethical, executive); he delivers hard truths cleanly and expects the same from others. His shadow is mistaking his principles for the whole of the truth.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the King of Swords signals a principled, reliable, sometimes austere partner — or the version of you showing up with clarity about the terms of the relationship. Real, durable, not always romantic.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours law, medicine, ethics, executive authority, and any role where principled judgement and clear communication are the deliverable.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the King supports clear, ethical, structured money decisions — contracts honoured, terms fair, decisions grounded in principle not appetite.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: cold, rule-bound, principled to the point of cruelty, delivering verdicts without listening.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: tyrannical leadership, weaponized rules, decisions made without consulting those they affect.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: contracts used as weapons, ethical shortcuts dressed up as principle.

best_overall.name: Lapis Lazuli
best_overall.reason: Lapis Lazuli (Wisdom / Truth / Inner Vision) is the King's principled-authority stone — sovereign judgement grounded in clear seeing and ethical structure.

best_upright.name: Sodalite
best_upright.reason: Sodalite (Logic / Honesty) anchors the King's judgements in honest, rigorous reasoning.

best_reversed.name: Amethyst
best_reversed.reason: When authority becomes tyranny, Amethyst (Calm / Spiritual awareness) returns humility to the intellect and cools the dogmatic edge.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; principle softened by warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Selenite
best_daily_wear.reason: Selenite worn daily keeps the King's mind clear of the seduction of his own certainty.

cards.name: Page of Pentacles
cards.court_role_meaning: Page = the apprentice phase of Earth — the student of the material world; the youth stands in a green field holding a single pentacle with careful attention, the apprentice at the start of learning a craft, a trade, a body of practical knowledge.
reversed_keywords: not showing up for the work
cards.upright_meaning: A youth stands alone in a green field, holding a single pentacle up with the careful attention of a student regarding the object of his study — not chasing it, not yet skilled with it, but committing to learn. The Page of Pentacles is the apprentice of Earth, the part of you willing to be a beginner at something real — a craft, a trade, a savings plan, a business, a body of expertise. Upright, this card speaks to the diligence and hope of a genuine start, the moment you choose a concrete goal and commit to learning how to build it.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same apprentice energy scatters — procrastination, lack of follow-through, ambitions too big to fit the work you are actually doing, wanting the title without the trade. The shadow is not the ambition itself; it is the refusal to be a beginner. The card invites you to choose one concrete goal small enough to actually start this week.
cards.psychological_lens: implementation intention + the willingness to be a beginner; the gap between ambition and consistent practice
cards.eastern_anchor: The Page of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian ideal of '匠人精神' (craftsman spirit, shokunin) — the willingness to devote years of humble practice to mastering a single craft. Green Aventurine, the stone of opportunity and growth, pairs with this apprentice's hopeful, attentive beginning.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an aventurine; name one concrete skill or goal you have been calling too small to start. Take one real step toward it this week.
cards.as_feelings: If Page of Pentacles represents how someone feels about you: they feel a steady, hopeful, slightly earnest interest — they want to build something with you and are willing to learn how, even if they are clearly at the start.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Page of Pentacles is studious, ambitious, hard-working when focused, often young or newly committed to a path; they take their goals seriously and need help translating ambition into consistent practice.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Page of Pentacles signals a steady, earnest, building energy — a relationship (or a potential partner) willing to do the slow work of foundation, not just the chemistry.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours study, apprenticeship, starting a new venture or skill, and any path that rewards consistent diligent practice.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Page is one of the most encouraging cards in the deck — the start of a savings plan, a new income stream, a financial education. Concrete, achievable, real.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: someone all-talk about commitment without the steady work, or feeling too small to be worth the effort.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: procrastination, scattered goals, wanting the credential without the craft.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: unrealistic financial daydreams, not following the budget, ambitions without a plan.

cards.name: Knight of Pentacles
cards.court_role_meaning: Knight = the quest phase of Earth — but Earth's quest is the slowest in the deck; the knight sits on a heavy workhorse at a standstill, pentacle held in both hands, no charge, no rearing — relentless consistent progress at a pace nothing can rush.
cards.upright_meaning: The Knight of Pentacles sits on a heavy workhorse — the only knight in the deck whose horse is at a standstill, because Earth's quest is the slow one. His pentacle is held in both hands, his eyes are on the work, and his pace is the pace of someone who has understood that this particular mountain is climbed one steady step at a time. Upright, this card is reliability, consistency, and the unglamorous daily work that actually builds a life, a business, or a body of expertise.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same steadiness stalls — routine becomes rigidity, patience becomes stuckness, diligence becomes the avoidance of necessary risk, perfectionism paralyzing progress. The shadow is not the slowness itself; it is slowness used as a cover for fear. The card invites the Knight to ask what he has been calling 'being thorough' that is actually 'being afraid to finish'.
cards.psychological_lens: conscientiousness at the highest tier; the difference between diligent consistency and rigid perfectionism
cards.eastern_anchor: The Knight of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian virtue of '愚公移山' (the foolish old man who moves the mountain) — the patient, multi-generational persistence that accomplishes what speed cannot. Hematite, a stone of grounding and endurance, pairs with this steady, unhurriable force.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a hematite at the root; name one project you have been 'perfecting' to avoid finishing. Define the smallest version of done, and commit to it.
cards.as_feelings: If Knight of Pentacles represents how someone feels about you: they feel steady, committed, slow-building — not the chemistry of a spark but the durability of someone who shows up every day. Reliable, occasionally unromantic.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Knight of Pentacles is hard-working, reliable, patient, often traditional, allergic to shortcuts; he finishes what he starts and may resist necessary change. He is the partner, employee, or friend you can count on completely and may occasionally need to push.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Knight of Pentacles signals steady, committed, slow-building love — someone who shows up consistently rather than dramatically. Less spark, more foundation.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours the steady job done well over time, the reliable employee, the long-horizon builder. Excellent for momentum that compounds.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Knight is one of the most encouraging cards — consistent saving, slow compounding, the patient building of real wealth.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: stuck, routine-bound, the relationship on autopilot, resistant to growth or change.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: stuck in a dead-end, rigid routine, perfectionism preventing completion, fear of change.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: hoarding without growth, fear-based financial rigidity, refusing to invest in necessary change.

cards.name: Queen of Pentacles
cards.court_role_meaning: Queen = the mastery-and-containment phase of Earth — abundance has been internalized into nurturing care; she sits on a throne decorated with fruits and angels, a rabbit at her feet (fertility), her pentacle cradled in her lap with both hands, the figure who has learned to hold resources in service of life.
cards.upright_meaning: The Queen of Pentacles sits on a throne carved with fruits and angels, a rabbit at her feet (the symbol of fertility), her pentacle resting in her lap cradled by both hands — she is the figure who has learned to hold resources not to hoard but to nourish. Behind her, mountains and a field of green. Upright, this card is the part of you (or someone in your life) that nurtures through practical care — a warm home, a fed body, a stocked pantry, a held budget, a hand on the shoulder of someone who needs grounding.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same nurturing collapses — giving until empty, mothering that becomes smothering, providing that becomes self-neglect, practical care that hides material anxiety. The shadow is not the care itself; it is care without self-inclusion. The card invites the Queen to ask whose needs she has been meeting at the cost of her own.
cards.psychological_lens: nurturance + practical stewardship; the difference between sustainable care and self-abandoning provision
cards.eastern_anchor: The Queen of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian image of the '地母' (Earth Mother) — the feminine principle that nourishes all things without depletion. Green Aventurine, the stone of growth and opportunity, pairs with her abundant, nurturing energy — and Crystal Coven names it her stone.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an aventurine at the heart; list three things you have provided for others this week. Add one thing you will provide for yourself.
cards.as_feelings: If Queen of Pentacles represents how someone feels about you: they feel held, provided for, practically cared for — a love that shows up as dinner on the table, money in the account, a hand on your back when you are tired. Durable, embodied warmth.
cards.as_person: As a person, the Queen of Pentacles is warm, practical, financially grounded, a caregiver by nature, often a parent or the heart of a household or business; she holds resources in service of life and risks holding too much. Her growth edge is receiving as gracefully as she gives.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Queen of Pentacles signals nurturing, practical, financially grounded partnership — the version of love that builds a home, not just a feeling. Excellent omen for family and long-term commitment.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours roles combining care with practical stewardship — management that nurtures, business with a heart, finance in service of life.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Queen supports grounded, nurturing financial care — budgeting that serves life, abundance that is shared, security that is built without anxiety.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: smothering, self-neglect, the relationship where one partner disappears into providing for the other.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: overwork, work-life imbalance, providing for the team to the point of burnout.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: material anxiety, hoarding out of fear, providing beyond your means.

best_overall.name: Green Aventurine
best_overall.reason: Green Aventurine (Earth, Opportunity / Growth / Confidence) is the Queen's nurturing-abundance stone — growth held in service of life; Crystal Coven Pentacles consensus pick.

best_upright.reason: Rose Quartz (Heart, compassion) deepens the Queen's warm, nurturing heart.

best_reversed.reason: When nurturing becomes self-neglect, Red Jasper (Grounding / Strength / Endurance) returns the Queen to her own body and replenishes her stamina.

cards.name: King of Pentacles
cards.court_role_meaning: King = the mastery-and-mastery phase of Earth — abundance has been built and is now held as legacy; he sits throneed in robes of abundance-luxuriant grapes and vines, his pentacle footed on the earth, the bull of his element carved beneath him — the figure who has fully mastered the material world and now offers it outward as provision, structure, and inheritance.
cards.upright_meaning: The King of Pentacles sits on a throne carved with bull motifs (his element's creature) and draped in robes woven with grapevines (the luxury of cultivated abundance); his pentacle is footed firmly on the earth, and his posture is the settled ease of someone who has actually built what he stands on. Where the Queen nurtures, the King builds and provides at scale. Upright, this card is mastered manifestation — wealth held wisely, success used to provision others, legacy built on solid ground, the embodiment of 'enough and to spare'.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same abundance hardens — greed, hoarding, money used to control, workaholism at the cost of relationship, abundance without generosity, success that has become its own prison. The shadow is not the wealth itself; it is wealth holding the King rather than the King holding it. The card invites him to ask what his abundance is in service of beyond himself.
cards.psychological_lens: stewardship + mastery of the material; the difference between wealth as resource and wealth as identity
cards.eastern_anchor: The King of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian image of the '财神' (God of Wealth) at his most principled — abundance as a mandate to provision others, not to hoard. Tiger's Eye, the stone Crystal Coven names for the King of Pentacles, pairs with the clarity, courage, and protection required to hold significant wealth without being corrupted by it.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a tiger-eye stone; name one form of abundance you have mastered (money, time, skill, network). Name one person or cause beyond yourself it could provision this month.
cards.as_feelings: If King of Pentacles represents how someone feels about you: they feel provided for, protected, and held by someone who has built something real — a love expressed through stability, provision, and the willingness to be a foundation you can stand on.
cards.as_person: As a person, the King of Pentacles is successful, generous when healthy, financially grounded, often a business owner or in a position of mature authority; he embodies 'enough and to spare' and risks identifying with what he has built. His growth edge is holding wealth loosely enough to enjoy it.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the King of Pentacles signals stable, providing, committed partnership — the version of love that shows up as a mortgage paid, a future planned, a hand that holds steady through hard seasons. Excellent for long-term commitment and family.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card marks the peak of material mastery — successful business ownership, executive leadership, financial authority, legacy-building work.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the King of Pentacles is one of the most encouraging cards in the deck — mastered wealth, stable provision, long-term financial security built on solid ground.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: controlling through money, materialism, workaholism at the cost of intimacy, provision without presence.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: greed, ruthless business practices, success that has become its own prison, hoarding rather than delegating.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: greed, hoarding, money controlling the man, abundance disconnected from generosity.

best_overall.reason: Tiger's Eye (Courage / Clarity / Protection / Determination) is the King's mastered-wealth stone — the clarity and protection to hold significant resources without being corrupted; Crystal Coven Pentacles consensus pick.

best_reversed.reason: When abundance hardens into hoarding, Aventurine (Growth / Opportunity) reminds the King that wealth is meant to flow and provision growth, not just accumulate.

best_daily_wear.reason: Jade worn daily supports wise, harmonious stewardship of abundance across a long horizon.

cards.name: Ace of Wands
cards.progression_meaning: spark / seed / new inspiration — the raw gift of Fire arriving fresh; the moment passion first lights before it has been shaped into direction
cards.progression_position: 1 = the Ace — the suit's source; in Wands, the spark of passion, creative inspiration, or the will to begin that everything else in the suit will develop
cards.upright_meaning: A single hand emerges from a cloud holding a living wand upright; green leaves sprout from its tip in a barren landscape — the raw gift of Fire arriving fresh in your hands, before it has been planned, named, or shaped into anything. The Ace of Wands is the spark: a new passion, a creative inspiration, the sudden 'yes' that lights you up. Upright, this card is the gift of energy to begin — pure, unshaped, asking to be tended into a flame.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same spark is dampened — delayed inspiration, low energy, a creative impulse that has not found its follow-through, self-doubt smothering the 'yes' before it can grow. The gift is still being offered; it is simply blocked, mis-timed, or not yet claimed. This is not a verdict on your fire; it is an invitation to clear what is smothering the spark.
cards.psychological_lens: the activated approach system + the dopamine of new possibility; the threshold between inspiration and implementation
cards.eastern_anchor: The Ace of Wands resonates with the Tibetan image of '菩提心' (bodhicitta, the first arising of aspiration on the path) — the spark of awakening before it has become a practice. Carnelian, the stone historically carried as a Fire-starter across Eastern and Western traditions, pairs with this freshly lit, unshaped spark.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a carnelian in your dominant hand; speak aloud one spark of passion or creative inspiration you have felt recently. Name one small action that would tend it into a flame this week.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Ace of Wands heralds a spark of attraction — chemistry, sexual energy, the electric 'yes' of new passion. Often the start of something, not yet the foundation.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card is the new idea, the inspiration for a venture or project, the burst of motivation to begin. Excellent for creative and entrepreneurial starts.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Ace can signal a new income impulse — a side-hustle idea, a creative venture, an opportunity with potential. Tend it with a plan.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: chemistry without follow-through, spark that fades, low sexual energy, attraction that does not mature.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: false starts, inspiration without execution, energy spread across too many sparks.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: ideas that never ship, enthusiasm that fades before income arrives.

best_overall.name: Carnelian
best_overall.reason: Carnelian (Fire, Courage / Motivation / Creativity) is the Ace's spark-stone — the raw gift of Fire arriving fresh; the natural Starter stone of the Wands suit.

best_upright.name: Citrine
best_upright.reason: Citrine (Fire, Abundance / Motivation / Confidence) amplifies the spark's warmth and the will to tend it.

best_reversed.name: Sunstone
best_reversed.reason: When the spark is dampened, Sunstone (Joy / Warmth / Confidence) re-lights optimism and vitality.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger's Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger's Eye worn daily protects the new spark and gives courage to tend it into flame.
