cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Carnelian chosen best_overall per Wands Fire-element consensus (Ace = spark, Carnelian = spark-starter).

cards.name: Ace of Cups
cards.suit_theme: emotion / intuition / relationship / creativity of the heart
cards.progression_meaning: emotional awakening / love beginning / intuition surfacing — the raw gift of Water arriving fresh; the cup offered full, the heart cracked open to receive
cards.progression_position: 1 = the Ace — the suit's source; in Cups, the awakening of feeling, intuition, or love; the cup offered full from a cloud, the wellspring before it has been poured into anything
cards.upright_meaning: A hand emerges from a cloud holding an overflowing cup; water pours from it in five streams (the five senses) into a pond where lotus flowers bloom — a dove descends bearing a host, the symbol of spiritual communion. The Ace of Cups is the wellspring: the heart cracked open, feeling and intuition rising fresh, the gift of love arriving unasked. Upright, this card is emotional awakening — new love, spiritual opening, compassion freshly flowing, the moment the cup is offered full.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same cup is blocked — feeling repressed, intuition ignored, love delayed, the heart closed against what is being offered. The gift is still there; it is simply not flowing yet. This is not a verdict on your capacity to feel; it is an invitation to notice what your heart is currently bracing against.
cards.psychological_lens: openness to feeling + the thawing of emotional defences; the moment a closed system opens to receive
cards.eastern_anchor: The Ace of Cups resonates with the East Asian image of '甘露' (sweet dew) and the lotus rising from muddy water (出淤泥而不染) — purity emerging from the depths. Moonstone, sacred across Indian and East Asian traditions as the stone of the receptive lunar principle (Chandra / Soma), pairs with this fresh, overflowing water of feeling.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a moonstone at the heart; name one feeling you have been holding back. Allow yourself to feel it fully for one minute, without acting on it yet.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Ace of Cups is one of the most beautiful cards in the deck — new love, an emotional awakening, the moment the heart opens to someone. Often the literal beginning of a relationship or the deepening of one into real intimacy.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card signals the heart engaging with the work — a calling found, a creative project birthed, an emotional sense of purpose entering what you do.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Ace can signal emotionally aligned financial decisions or a new income source tied to heartfelt work; beware of spending out of pure emotion.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: emotional block, the heart closed, love offered but not yet able to be received, intimacy withheld.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: creative drought, the heart disengaged, work without emotional connection.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: emotionally driven spending, financial decisions made from feeling rather than grounding.

best_overall.name: Moonstone
best_overall.reason: Moonstone (Water, Intuition / New Beginnings / Calm) is the Ace's wellspring-stone — the fresh, receptive principle of Water arriving; the natural Starter stone of the Cups suit.

best_upright.name: Rose Quartz
best_upright.reason: Rose Quartz (Heart, unconditional love) amplifies the Ace's gift of love flowing fresh.

best_reversed.name: Aquamarine
best_reversed.reason: When the heart closes, Aquamarine (Water, Calm / Communication / Courage) gently supports the surfacing and voicing of repressed feeling.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; the Ace of love amplified.

best_daily_wear.name: Blue Lace Agate
best_daily_wear.reason: Blue Lace Agate worn daily supports calm, gentle, honest expression of feeling — keeping the cup open.

cards.related_reason: Ace is the suit's source, leading to Two of Cups (the first partnership); Page of Cups is the human apprentice echo; The High Priestess (Major 2) shares the intuitive-unconscious theme; The Star (Major 17) shares the spiritual-renewal-through-water theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Moonstone chosen best_overall per Cups Water-element consensus (Ace = wellspring, Moonstone = receptive lunar water).

cards.name: Ace of Swords
cards.progression_meaning: mental breakthrough / clear insight / truth arriving — the raw gift of Air arriving fresh; the sword offered upright, the mind suddenly seeing through what was murky
cards.progression_position: 1 = the Ace — the suit's source; in Swords, the moment of mental clarity, breakthrough insight, or the cutting-through of illusion; the sword offered upright from a cloud with a crown of victory
cards.upright_meaning: A hand emerges from a cloud holding a single upright sword; at its tip a crown hangs surrounded by palm and bay leaves (victory and triumph of the mind), and the sky is clear. The Ace of Swords is the breakthrough — the moment the mind suddenly sees through what was murky, the truth arrives, the sharp idea cuts clean. Upright, this card is the gift of clarity: a new insight, a moment of honest seeing, the courage to name what is true and act on it.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same clarity is clouded — confusion, misinformation, a brilliant idea misused, the truth delayed, a mental block standing between you and the seeing you need. The gift is still offered; it is simply obscured. This is not a verdict on your intelligence; it is an invitation to clear what is clouding the lens.
cards.psychological_lens: insight + cognitive clarity; the moment a confused system suddenly reorganizes around a sharper truth
cards.eastern_anchor: The Ace of Swords resonates with the Tibetan image of Manjushri's sword (慧剑) — the blade of discriminating wisdom that cuts through delusion in a single stroke. Selenite, named for selēnē (the moon) and used across contemplative traditions to clear the mind, pairs with this clean, single stroke of clarity.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a selenite wand at the third eye; name one area of your life where your thinking has been clouded. Ask what single truth, if you let yourself see it clearly, would reorganize the confusion.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Ace of Swords signals clarity about a relationship — a moment of honest seeing, a truth spoken, a decision made with eyes open. Less about chemistry, more about honesty.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card is the breakthrough idea, the moment of clear strategy, the courage to name what is true in the organization. Excellent for decisions, justice, advocacy.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Ace supports clear financial seeing — reading the contract honestly, understanding the numbers, cutting through confusion to a sound decision.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: confusion, misinformation, a relationship clouded by unspoken truths, honesty delayed.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: muddled thinking, brilliant ideas misused, decisions based on bad information.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: confusion in contracts, misinformation driving decisions, truth obscured.

best_overall.name: Selenite
best_overall.reason: Selenite (Air, Clarity / Cleansing / Calm) is the Ace's breakthrough-stone — the clean blade of clear seeing; the natural Starter stone of the Swords suit.

best_upright.name: Amethyst
best_upright.reason: Amethyst (Air, Calm / Spiritual awareness / Protection) deepens the Ace's clarity into steady, sober seeing.

best_reversed.name: Fluorite
best_reversed.reason: When thinking is clouded, Fluorite (Air, Focus / Clarity / Decision) helps organize scattered thought into a clear next step.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; clarity softened by warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Lapis Lazuli
best_daily_wear.reason: Lapis worn daily supports wise, truthful seeing as an ongoing practice.

cards.related_reason: Ace is the suit's source, leading to Two of Swords (the first decision); Page of Swords is the human apprentice echo; Justice (Major 11) shares the truth-verdict theme; The Magician (Major 1) shares the focused-will theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Selenite chosen best_overall per Swords Air-element consensus (Ace = breakthrough, Selenite = clarity-cleanser).

cards.name: Ace of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: material opportunity / financial new beginning / the seed of abundance — the raw gift of Earth arriving fresh; the pentacle offered solid and whole, a tangible opportunity in your hands
cards.progression_position: 1 = the Ace — the suit's source; in Pentacles, a concrete material opportunity, a financial new start, a tangible gift of resources or health; the pentacle offered from a cloud into a garden of lilies and roses
cards.upright_meaning: A hand emerges from a cloud holding a single solid pentacle; below it lies a garden of lilies and roses — cultivated abundance, fertility grounded in the real. The Ace of Pentacles is the seed of material opportunity: a new venture, a financial beginning, an offer of work or money or health that you can actually touch. Upright, this card is the gift of the material arriving in your hands — concrete, real, asking to be planted and tended.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same opportunity is missed or delayed — the offer not taken, the abundance not trusted, the false start, the bad investment, the scarcity mindset that cannot receive what is being given. The gift is still there; it is simply not being claimed. This is not a verdict on your worthiness; it is an invitation to ask what is keeping your hand closed when something is being offered.
cards.psychological_lens: scarcity vs abundance mindset + the willingness to receive; the gap between opportunity and uptake
cards.eastern_anchor: The Ace of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian image of the '金元宝' (gold ingot) offered at New Year — abundance as a fresh, tangible gift requiring cultivation. Green Aventurine, traditionally the stone of opportunity and good fortune, pairs with this seed of material possibility.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an aventurine at the heart or palm; name one concrete opportunity currently being offered to you. Decide whether you will accept it, and one step you will take this week if you do.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Ace of Pentacles signals a relationship with real-world foundation — a partnership that builds, buys, plans, and roots. Less about chemistry, more about the tangible future you could build together.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card is one of the most encouraging in the deck — a new job, a raise, a venture, a contract, a tangible business opportunity. Real and takeable.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Ace of Pentacles is the strongest possible card — a new income stream, a financial opportunity, the seed of real abundance. Plant it well.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: an opportunity not taken, a foundation not built, a partner who cannot receive what is being offered.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: missed opportunity, false start, the offer declined out of scarcity or fear.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: missed financial opportunity, bad investment, scarcity mindset blocking abundance.

best_overall.name: Green Aventurine
best_overall.reason: Green Aventurine (Earth, Opportunity / Growth / Confidence) is the Ace's seed-of-abundance stone — the gift of the material arriving fresh; the natural Starter stone of the Pentacles suit.
best_overall.source: 390 lib Element=Earth + Intentions; Crystal Coven Pentacles consensus

best_upright.name: Jade
best_upright.reason: Jade (Earth, Harmony / Wisdom / Good fortune) deepens the Ace's long-horizon good fortune.

best_reversed.name: Pyrite
best_reversed.reason: When abundance is blocked or the offer not taken, Pyrite (Abundance / Will / Protection) reactivates the will to receive and steward wealth.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; tangible foundation with warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Red Jasper
best_daily_wear.reason: Red Jasper worn daily grounds the new opportunity in steady, enduring action.

cards.related_reason: Ace is the suit's source, leading to Two of Pentacles (the first balancing of resources); Page of Pentacles is the human apprentice echo; The Emperor (Major 4) and The Empress (Major 3) share the material-foundation and abundance themes.
cards.source_notes: Crystal Coven Pentacles consensus picks Aventurine for Ace of Pentacles; we adopt. Research §1.1 word #5 confirms this card has the most active crystals-for-cross SERP of the Aces.

cards.name: Two of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: balancing resources / flexible scheduling / juggling commitments — the moment the Ace's seed splits into two real demands and you must keep both in motion
cards.progression_position: 2 = the first real test of Earth's gift: the seed has sprouted into two competing claims on your time, money, or energy, and the practice is to keep them both aloft without dropping either
cards.upright_meaning: A young man juggles two pentacles in an infinite loop, his body bent like the lemniscate (the infinity symbol) above his head; behind him two ships ride a choppy sea — the world keeps moving while he keeps both coins in the air. The Two of Pentacles is the card of practiced juggling: the moment Earth's gift has multiplied into two real demands and the body has found a rhythm that can hold both. Upright, this card honours genuine adaptability — you are managing competing priorities (work and family, two projects, income and study) and finding the flow that lets neither drop.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same juggling becomes overcommitment — too many plates in the air, the rhythm broken, the body running on fumes, the ships behind him threatening to capsize. The shadow is not the adaptability itself; it is the refusal to admit that two coins have become five and the hands cannot hold them all. The card invites you to ask which commitment you are juggling out of fear of letting go rather than genuine capacity to hold.
cards.psychological_lens: cognitive flexibility + workload management; the difference between adaptive multitasking and reactive overcommitment
cards.eastern_anchor: The Two of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian image of '太极' (taiji, the taijitu) — two forces in continuous, balanced motion, neither fixed, each defining the other. Green Aventurine, the stone of opportunity and flow, pairs with this flexible, in-motion balancing of Earth's gifts.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an aventurine in your non-dominant hand; list every commitment you are currently juggling. Cross out one that is being held by fear rather than by genuine capacity.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Two of Pentacles signals a relationship (or dating life) being balanced against competing demands — work, family, two potential partners. The feeling is real but stretched; honesty about capacity matters now.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card points to managing multiple projects, roles, or income streams — genuine adaptability if the rhythm holds, scattering if it does not.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Two of Pentacles describes cash-flow juggling — money in and out at a pace that requires active management. Budget the rhythm, not just the total.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: a partner stretched too thin, the relationship losing to work, or dating two people without honesty to either.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: overcommitment, dropped balls, burnout, the cost of saying yes to everything.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: cash-flow crisis, missed payments, money scattered across too many obligations.

best_overall.name: Green Aventurine
best_overall.reason: Aventurine (Earth, Opportunity / Growth / Confidence) is the juggler's stone — flexible, in-motion abundance that helps the body hold two demands without rigidity.

best_upright.name: Jade
best_upright.reason: Jade (Earth, Harmony / Wisdom) supports the harmonious, long-horizon balance of competing demands.

best_reversed.name: Hematite
best_reversed.reason: When juggling scatters into overcommitment, Hematite (Grounding / Strength) returns the body to the earth and the nervous system to a manageable pace.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; keeps the relationship from being lost in the juggle.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger's Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger's Eye worn daily supports clear prioritisation and protects against spreading too thin.

cards.related_reason: Ace→Two progression in Pentacles (seed to first juggle); Three of Pentacles is the collaboration that follows; Eight of Pentacles is the mastery the juggling eventually focuses into; Temperance (Major 14) shares the active-balancing theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Aventurine chosen best_overall per earth-suit consensus (Two of Pentacles = juggling resources, Aventurine = flexible opportunity). Research §3.3 Pentacles 2 = 'balance resources / 灵活调度'.

cards.name: Three of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: teamwork / skilled collaboration / first tangible build — the seed has become a real project and now requires more than one pair of hands
cards.progression_position: 3 = the early-expansion node of Earth: the juggling of the Two settles into a shared build, the moment resources combine with other people's skills into a tangible, growing structure
upright_keywords: apprentice and master working side by side
cards.upright_meaning: In an archway under construction, an architect, a monk, and an apprentice stand over a blueprint; the apprentice on the bench holds up his work for review, and the three are negotiating the build together — each bringing a different skill to a shared structure. The Three of Pentacles is the card of skilled collaboration: the moment Earth's gift moves from solo juggling to a real team, the first tangible output that requires more than one pair of hands. Upright, this card honours the dignity of combined craft — your skill meeting another's skill meeting a third's vision, building something none of you could build alone.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same collaboration frays — team members working at cross-purposes, skill mismatch, ego battles over the blueprint, the build delayed by people who cannot agree on the foundation. The shadow is not the teamwork itself; it is the failure to align skill with role and vision with contribution. The card invites you to ask which person on the team (including yourself) is in the wrong seat, and what conversation has been avoided that the build needs.
cards.psychological_lens: complementary roles + shared mental models; the difference between synergy and friction in a working group
cards.eastern_anchor: The Three of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian ideal of '三人行必有我师' (wherever three walk together, one must be my teacher, Confucius) — the dignity of learning and building across roles and ranks. Jade, the stone of harmony and wise collaboration across Han and Tibetan traditions, pairs with this skilled, shared build.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a jade stone; name one person whose skill complements yours on a current project. Speak one honest appreciation of their contribution to them this week.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Three of Pentacles signals a relationship being actively built together — shared goals, complementary roles, the willingness to negotiate the blueprint of a shared life. A practical, durable foundation.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card is one of the strongest teamwork omens — skilled collaboration, being valued for your contribution, a project that genuinely needs what you bring.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Three supports income earned through collaboration, partnership ventures, or skilled work that requires a team — the first tangible pay-off of combined effort.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: working at cross-purposes in the relationship, one partner carrying the build, mismatched visions of the future.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: team disharmony, feeling undervalued for your skill, projects delayed by poor collaboration.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: partnership disputes over money, projects that stall before paying out, contribution not recognised.

best_overall.name: Jade
best_overall.reason: Jade (Earth, Harmony / Wisdom / Good fortune) is the collaborative-build stone — the harmony required for skilled hands to build together without ego friction.

best_upright.name: Green Aventurine
best_upright.reason: Aventurine (Opportunity / Growth) opens the team to the opportunity that combined skills create.

best_reversed.name: Pyrite
best_reversed.reason: When collaboration frays into ego battle, Pyrite (Abundance / Will / Protection) re-anchors each member's will to the shared build rather than personal credit.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; shared build with warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Red Jasper
best_daily_wear.reason: Red Jasper worn daily gives the steady stamina that real collaboration requires.

cards.related_reason: Two→Three progression in Pentacles (juggle to team); Eight of Pentacles is the solo mastery that follows the team-build; Ten of Pentacles is the legacy the build compounds into; The Hierophant (Major 5) shares the apprentice-master / shared-tradition theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Jade chosen best_overall per earth-suit consensus (Three of Pentacles = teamwork / harmony, Jade = harmony stone). Research §3.3 Pentacles 3 = '团队 / 学徒 / 协作建造'.

cards.name: Six of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: generosity / giving and receiving / balanced exchange — the harmony node of Earth, where resources flow in a healthy two-way current
cards.progression_position: 6 = the harmony/giving node of Earth's progression: the build of the Three has stabilised, and now abundance flows outward and inward in balanced measure — the merchant weighing coins on his scale, giving without depletion
cards.upright_meaning: A wealthy merchant in a red robe stands holding a balance; with one hand he drops coins into the beggars' palms, with the other he holds the scale that ensures the giving is measured and sustainable. The Six of Pentacles is the card of balanced generosity: abundance that has stabilised enough to flow outward, the harmony of giving and receiving in fair measure. Upright, this card honours healthy reciprocity — you are in a position to give (time, money, mentorship) in a way that empowers rather than traps, or you are receiving help with the dignity that keeps the exchange clean.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same generosity distorts — giving with strings attached, debt weaponised into obligation, charity that consolidates power rather than empowering, or the withholding of help that could easily be given. The shadow is not the giving itself; it is the imbalance hidden inside it. The card invites you to ask, whether you are the giver or the receiver, where the power in this exchange actually sits, and what it would take to make the flow genuinely two-way.
cards.psychological_lens: reciprocal altruism + the power dynamics of exchange; the difference between empowering help and help that creates dependency
cards.eastern_anchor: The Six of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian image of '舍得' (shědé — the wisdom that to give is to receive, that holding loosely creates the flow) and the Buddhist practice of '布施' (dāna, generous giving without expectation of return). Green Aventurine, the stone of opportunity and abundant flow, pairs with this balanced, life-serving generosity.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an aventurine; name one exchange in your life that has become one-sided. Decide one concrete act — giving, receiving, or rebalancing — that would restore the flow this week.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Six of Pentacles signals balanced reciprocity — a relationship where giving and receiving flow in healthy measure, or the moment to rebalance an exchange that has become one-sided. Generosity with dignity on both sides.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card favours mentorship, fair compensation, patronage, and roles where your contribution is genuinely valued. A good omen for recognition that matches effort.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Six supports generosity that you can sustain (give within your means), fair lending, and the receiving of help that empowers rather than indebts. Avoid loans that create obligation you cannot hold.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: one partner over-giving or over-receiving, strings attached to generosity, love used as currency.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: underpaid work, exploitation, mentorship that extracts, or compensation that does not match contribution.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: debt creating power imbalance, lending what you cannot afford to lose, or selfish withholding of help you owe.

best_overall.name: Green Aventurine
best_overall.reason: Aventurine (Earth, Opportunity / Growth / Abundance) is the generosity-stone — the abundant flow that gives without depletion and keeps the scale balanced.

best_upright.name: Jade
best_upright.reason: Jade (Harmony / Wisdom / Good fortune) deepens the harmony and wisdom that balanced exchange requires.

best_reversed.name: Rose Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When giving distorts into strings-attached exchange, Rose Quartz (Heart, unconditional love) restores the warmth that empowers rather than obligates.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; reciprocity with warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Pyrite
best_daily_wear.reason: Pyrite worn daily protects against the will-distortion that imbalanced exchange creates, and re-anchors abundance in integrity.

cards.related_reason: Five→Six progression in Pentacles (scarcity to giving); Ten of Pentacles is the legacy the flow compounds into; Ace of Pentacles is the suit's source; The Empress (Major 3) shares the abundant-nurturing-outward theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Aventurine chosen best_overall per earth-suit consensus (Six of Pentacles = generosity, Aventurine = abundant flow). Research §3.3 Pentacles 6 = '慷慨 / 给予 / 互助'.

cards.name: Seven of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: patience / assessing slow growth / waiting for the harvest — the pause to evaluate whether the long effort is bearing fruit
cards.progression_position: 7 = the assessment/patience node of Earth's progression: the build has been going long enough to assess — the gardener leans on his hoe, six pentacles ripe on the vine and one still green, and asks whether the effort is worth continuing
cards.upright_meaning: A farmer in a blue tunic leans on his hoe, gazing at the six pentacles heavy on his vine while a seventh remains green at the top; his posture is the patient pause of someone who has put in real work and is now waiting to see if it bears fruit. The Seven of Pentacles is the card of patient assessment: the long-horizon effort has reached a moment of honest evaluation — is this crop worth continuing to tend, or has it been the wrong field all along? Upright, this card honours the maturity of slowing down enough to assess, to trust the pace of real growth, and to invest attention where the harvest is actually forming.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same patience curdles — impatience with the pace, abandoning the crop before the harvest, the frustration of effort that has been going to the wrong field, or the discovery that the wrong thing was planted. The shadow is not the waiting itself; it is the unwillingness to assess honestly whether the effort is matched to the soil. The card invites you to ask, without judgement, whether the field you are tending is actually capable of growing what you hope to harvest from it.
cards.psychological_lens: delayed gratification + outcome evaluation; the difference between patient persistence and sunk-cost denial
cards.eastern_anchor: The Seven of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian virtue of '功不唐捐' (no effort is in vain, from the Avatamsaka Sutra) — but balanced by the farmer's honest assessment of whether the field itself is right. Moss Agate, traditionally the gardener's stone across European and East Asian folk practice, pairs with this patient tending of slow growth.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a moss-agate stone; name one long effort you are currently assessing. Ask honestly — without abandoning it and without denying the evidence — whether the field is capable of growing what you hope to harvest.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Seven of Pentacles signals a relationship at a moment of honest assessment — is this partnership bearing the fruit you hoped for, and is the patient tending worth continuing? Not a verdict, a mature check-in.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card points to assessing the long game — a project, a job, a business — asking whether the patient effort is matched by real growth or whether the field itself needs to change.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Seven supports patient, long-horizon wealth-building and honest assessment of investments — but is not investment advice. Ask whether your strategy matches your timeframe and risk reality, and consider qualified counsel for major decisions.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: impatience with a relationship's pace, giving up before the harvest, or staying in a field that cannot grow what you need.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: frustration with slow progress, wasted effort, or the realisation you have been tending the wrong career.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: impatience undermining a sound long-term strategy, or stubbornly tending an investment that will not bear fruit. (Not investment advice — seek qualified counsel.)

best_overall.name: Moss Agate
best_overall.reason: Moss Agate (Earth, Growth / Abundance / New Beginnings) is the gardener's stone — the patient tending of slow growth and the honest assessment of what the soil can bear; its very name evokes the vine and the field.

best_upright.name: Jade
best_upright.reason: Jade (Harmony / Wisdom / Good fortune) deepens the long-horizon wisdom of patient growth.

best_reversed.name: Green Aventurine
best_reversed.reason: When impatience or a wrong crop is suspected, Aventurine (Opportunity / Growth) re-opens the eyes to where growth is actually possible.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; patient love with warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Red Jasper
best_daily_wear.reason: Red Jasper worn daily gives the steady endurance that long-horizon tending requires.

cards.related_reason: Six→Seven→Eight progression in Pentacles (give → assess → master); Nine of Pentacles is the self-sufficiency the patient garden eventually yields; The Star (Major 17) shares the patient-hope theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Moss Agate chosen best_overall for the direct gardener/vine symbolic match (the card's literal image is a farmer leaning on a hoe among vines) + Element=Earth + gardener's-stone tradition. Research §3.3 Pentacles 7 = '耐心 / 评估 / 等待收成'.

cards.name: Eight of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: mastery / diligent practice / deepening craft — the single-focus immersion that turns competent into skilled
cards.progression_position: 8 = the mastery/discipline node of Earth's progression: the assessment of the Seven has decided the field is right, and now the focus narrows to the dedicated, repetitive, deeply skilled work of becoming genuinely good at one thing
cards.upright_meaning: A craftsman sits on a bench at his work, hammer raised, six finished pentacles lined up neatly on the wall behind him and a seventh in progress before him; his whole attention is on the work, the town left behind as he gives himself to becoming skilled. The Eight of Pentacles is the card of devoted practice: the moment Earth's gift narrows from juggling and assessing into the single-focus immersion that turns competent into master. Upright, this card honours the dignity of deep work — showing up daily to one craft, repeating the stroke until it becomes skill, the patient apprenticeship that no shortcut can replace.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same devotion distorts — perfectionism that never ships, overwork without real progress, scattered dabbling across many skills without mastering any, or skilled work done in a vacuum without ever being offered to the world. The shadow is not the practice itself; it is practice used to avoid completion, or completion withheld out of fear of judgement. The card invites you to ask which of your skills you have been refining past the point where the world actually needs it delivered.
cards.psychological_lens: deliberate practice + deep work; the difference between mastery-oriented repetition and avoidance-driven perfectionism
cards.eastern_anchor: The Eight of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian ideal of '匠人精神' (shokunin / craftsman spirit) — the willingness to give decades of devoted practice to a single craft, refining the stroke until the hands know what the mind has stopped needing to direct. Red Jasper, traditionally a stone of endurance and steady stamina, pairs with this devoted, repetitive, body-learned craft.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a red-jasper stone; name one craft or skill you are currently deepening. Define the smallest version of done that would let the work be offered to someone this month.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Eight of Pentacles signals devoted attention to a relationship — the willingness to do the daily work of becoming skilled at loving this particular person. Less chemistry, more craft.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card is one of the strongest skill-building omens — diligent apprenticeship, dedicated practice, becoming genuinely good at your craft. Excellent for study, training, and the deep work of mastery.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Eight supports income earned through genuine skill and dedicated craft — the long, patient investment in becoming good at what pays. Solid, compounding, real.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: perfectionism toward a partner, working at the relationship without letting it be good enough, or withholding presence through over-focus elsewhere.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: perfectionism paralyzing completion, overwork without shipping, scattered skill-building without focus.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: refining a product past the point of return, skill not monetised, working hard without strategy.

best_overall.name: Red Jasper
best_overall.reason: Red Jasper (Earth, Grounding / Strength / Endurance) is the craftsman's stone — the steady stamina and body-learned repetition that devoted practice requires.

best_upright.name: Moss Agate
best_upright.reason: Moss Agate (Growth / Abundance) supports the slow, organic growth of skill over time.

best_reversed.name: Green Aventurine
best_reversed.reason: When devotion stalls into perfectionism or scattered dabbling, Aventurine (Opportunity / Growth) re-opens the focus to where real progress is possible.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; devoted craft with warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Hematite
best_daily_wear.reason: Hematite worn daily grounds the focused mind in the body and protects against overwork burnout.

cards.related_reason: Seven→Eight→Nine progression in Pentacles (assess → master → self-sufficiency); Three of Pentacles is the team build that preceded solo mastery; Nine of Pentacles is the self-sufficient harvest mastery yields; The Hermit (Major 9) shares the single-focus-inner-practice theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Red Jasper chosen best_overall per earth-suit consensus (Eight of Pentacles = diligent practice, Red Jasper = endurance). Research §3.3 Pentacles 8 = '精进 / 工艺 / 熟练'.

cards.name: Ten of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: completion / legacy / family wealth / generational abundance — the final node of Earth, where the long build becomes a foundation others stand on
cards.progression_position: 10 = the completion/legacy node of Earth's progression: the mastery of the Eight has compounded into settled abundance — three generations under one arch, the old man in robes surrounded by family, the pentacles arranged into a permanent structure
cards.upright_meaning: An elder in a richly patterned robe sits at the archway of his home; a younger couple and a child play with two dogs at his feet, and ten pentacles are arranged across the image like the Tree of Life — three generations, a settled home, the long build become a foundation. The Ten of Pentacles is the card of completed legacy: Earth's gift has compounded through patient work into something permanent — a home, a family, a body of work, a financial foundation that will outlast you. Upright, this card honours the deep satisfaction of arrival — the long effort that has become the ground the next generation stands on.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same legacy frays — financial instability, family disputes over inheritance or estate, the failure of a long build to culminate in security, or a legacy broken by poor stewardship. The shadow is not the legacy itself; it is the fragility or conflict hidden inside what looks settled. The card invites you to ask what the foundation of your 'arrival' actually rests on, and whether it is as permanent as it appears.
cards.psychological_lens: generativity + legacy orientation; the difference between wealth that endures and wealth that fractures across generations
cards.eastern_anchor: The Ten of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian ideal of '福禄寿' (the threefold blessing of fortune, status, and longevity, often shown as three generations under one roof) and the virtue of leaving a foundation for those who come after. Jade, the stone of good fortune and generational harmony across Han and Tibetan traditions, pairs with this lasting, family-rooted legacy.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a jade stone; name one foundation — financial, familial, or in your work — that you are building for those who come after you. Speak one act this month that strengthens what will outlast you.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Ten of Pentacles is one of the strongest commitment omens — marriage, family, a shared home, the long settlement of partnership into a foundation. Real arrival, not just a moment.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card marks the culmination of a long build — a business or body of work that has become a stable institution, a leadership legacy, an achievement that endures past the founder.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Ten of Pentacles is one of the most encouraging cards — long-term security, family wealth, settled assets, a financial foundation built to last. Pair with sound estate planning; not investment advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: family conflict undermining the relationship, a foundation that looks settled but is fragile, or commitment that fails to culminate in shared life.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: a legacy that fractures, institutional instability, a long build that fails to culminate in something durable.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: financial instability, estate disputes, legacy lost to poor stewardship. (Not investment advice — seek qualified counsel for major estate decisions.)

best_overall.name: Jade
best_overall.reason: Jade (Earth, Harmony / Wisdom / Good fortune) is the legacy stone — the generational harmony and lasting good fortune that the Ten of Pentacles represents; jade is historically the stone passed down across generations in both Han and Tibetan tradition.

best_upright.name: Pyrite
best_upright.reason: Pyrite (Abundance / Will / Protection) protects the legacy and anchors the will that holds wealth wisely rather than fragmenting it.

best_reversed.name: Green Aventurine
best_reversed.reason: When legacy frays, Aventurine (Opportunity / Growth) re-opens the path to rebuilding abundance on solid ground.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; family-rooted love with warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger's Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger's Eye worn daily supports clear stewardship and protects the foundation from being eroded.

cards.related_reason: Nine→Ten progression in Pentacles (self-sufficiency to legacy); Ace of Pentacles is the seed that grew into this; Six of Pentacles is the flow that fed it; King of Pentacles is the human figure who masters this energy; The World (Major 21) shares the completion-and-arrival theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Jade chosen best_overall per earth-suit consensus + generational-stone tradition (Ten of Pentacles = legacy / family wealth, Jade = the stone passed across generations). Research §3.3 Pentacles 10 = '传承 / 财富 / 家族'.

cards.name: Two of Wands
cards.progression_meaning: future planning / decision at the crossroads / stepping beyond comfort — the moment Fire's spark must choose a direction to burn toward
cards.progression_position: 2 = the choice/direction node of Fire's progression: the Ace's spark has been felt, and now the figure stands at the battlement holding a globe, asking 'now where?' — turning inspiration into a plan before any of it becomes action
cards.upright_meaning: A figure in a red robe stands at the battlement of his castle, holding a small globe in one hand while two wands frame the scene — one fixed to the wall beside him, one free in his grip. Behind him the cultivated land he already owns; before him the sea and distant hills he has not yet sailed toward. The Two of Wands takes the spark of the Ace and asks: now where? Upright, this card is the moment Fire's inspiration turns into genuine planning — the willingness to stand at the threshold of the known and the unknown, hold the whole world in your hand as possibility, and choose a direction to commit the next chapter of fire to.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same threshold becomes a wall — fear of the unknown, playing safe inside the castle you already own, the globe held but the ship never sailed, the plan never made. The shadow is not the caution itself; it is the refusal to admit which horizon you actually want, hidden behind the comfort of the known. The card invites you to ask what future you have been calling 'unrealistic' that is actually just outside your current map.
cards.psychological_lens: future orientation + implementation intention; the gap between vision and commitment, between holding the globe and sailing toward it
cards.eastern_anchor: The Two of Wands resonates with the Tibetan Buddhist stage of practice called 'lam-rim' (the graduated path) — the clear mapping of the journey before setting out, the honest assessment of where you are and where the path leads. Clear Quartz, historically called 'bosatsu-seki' (菩萨石, the Bodhisattva stone) in East Asian tradition and treasured for clarifying intention, pairs naturally with this path-mapping, threshold-clarifying quality.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a clear quartz point; name one direction you have been hesitating to commit to. Speak one concrete next step aloud — small enough to take this week.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Two of Wands suggests you are weighing a relationship's long-term horizon — or, if single, deciding whether to step toward someone or stay comfortable as you are. Real planning, not just chemistry.
cards.career_meaning: In career readings, this card signals you are planning your next professional move — a career switch, further education, overseas expansion, or the venture you have been holding at the planning stage. The map is being drawn.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Two of Wands encourages long-horizon planning — exploring investment horizons beyond your immediate comfort, mapping a financial strategy. This is strategic framing, not specific investment advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: hesitation, fear of commitment, staying in the comfort of the known, the relationship's future avoided.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: working without strategy, the plan never made, holding back from the move you actually want.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: avoiding long-term planning, staying in familiar but limited territory. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Clear Quartz
best_overall.reason: Clear Quartz (clarity / amplification of intention) is the threshold-clarifying stone — the path-mapping clarity that turns a held globe into a chosen direction; the research §4.2 Two of Wands sample explicitly pairs this card with Clear Quartz.

best_upright.name: Citrine
best_upright.reason: Citrine (Fire, Abundance / Motivation / Confidence) fuels the will to plan boldly and step toward the horizon.

best_reversed.name: Carnelian
best_reversed.reason: When fear of the unknown freezes the plan, Carnelian (Fire, Courage / Motivation) re-lights the courage to choose a direction.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; horizon weighed with warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger's Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger's Eye worn daily supports clear, courageous decision-making and protects against playing safe.

cards.related_reason: Ace→Two→Three progression in Wands (spark → plan → first sail); Page of Wands is the human apprentice echo of this threshold; The Magician (Major 1) shares the manifestation-intention theme; The Fool (Major 0) shares the leap-into-the-unknown theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Clear Quartz chosen best_overall per research §4.2 Two of Wands sample (explicitly pairs this card with 'bosatsu-seki'/Clear Quartz for path-mapping). ⚠️ 390 lib slug = quartz-meaning (NOT 'quartz' short form; Major tarot-knowledge.json used short 'quartz' which was wrong, corrected here).

cards.name: Two of Cups
cards.suit_theme: emotion / intuition / relationship / creativity of the heart
cards.progression_meaning: partnership / mutual attraction / two becoming a recognised pair — the first real joining after the heart's awakening
cards.progression_position: 2 = the partnership node of Water's progression: the Ace's wellspring has poured into a second vessel, and now two cups meet, two hearts recognise each other, the caduceus of balanced exchange between them
cards.upright_meaning: Two figures stand facing each other on a green hill, each holding a cup; between them the caduceus of Hermes rises with its twin serpents and lion-headed staff — the symbol of balanced exchange, of two energies meeting as equals. The Two of Cups is the card of mutual recognition: the moment Water's gift has poured into two vessels and the two genuinely see and choose each other. Upright, this card honours the chemistry of equals — romantic, platonic, or creative partnership where both bring a full cup and neither diminishes the other. It is one of the most beloved cards in the deck.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same union becomes imbalance — one cup fuller than the other, the truce between two people breaking, chemistry fading into distance, or the caduceus untwining as the exchange stops flowing both ways. The shadow is not the partnership itself; it is the imbalance hidden inside what looked like equals. The card invites you to ask where the flow between you and another has become one-directional, and what it would take to refill the lighter cup.
cards.psychological_lens: mutual attunement + secure reciprocity; the difference between a relationship of equals and one that merely performs equality
cards.eastern_anchor: The Two of Cups resonates with the East Asian image of '并蒂莲' (the twin lotus on one stalk) — two flowers sharing one root, the classical symbol of devoted partnership and recognised union. Rose Quartz, the house stone of the heart across both Western and Eastern traditions, pairs with this mutual, equal recognition.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a rose-quartz at the heart; name one relationship where the exchange has become unequal. Speak one honest sentence to that person this week that restores the flow.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Two of Cups is one of the most beautiful partnership cards — mutual attraction, a real meeting of equals, the beginning (or renewal) of a relationship where both people genuinely choose each other. Often the moment a connection becomes a recognised pair.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card signals a strong partnership — a collaborator, a co-founder, a working relationship of genuine mutual respect and complementary contribution.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Two of Cups supports balanced financial partnership — joint ventures, shared accounts, or contracts where both parties contribute and receive in fair measure.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: imbalance, a truce breaking down, chemistry fading, or a relationship where one is giving far more than the other.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: a partnership turning competitive, contribution unrecognised, a working relationship where the flow has stopped.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: financial partnership gone sour, joint venture where one carries the load, contracts that need rebalancing.

best_overall.name: Rose Quartz
best_overall.reason: Rose Quartz (Heart, unconditional love) is the mutual-recognition stone — the equal, two-way love the Two of Cups represents; the house stone of partnership.
best_overall.source: 390 lib Heart/love Intentions; water-suit + heart consensus

best_upright.name: Moonstone
best_upright.reason: Moonstone (Water, Intuition / New Beginnings) deepens the intuitive recognition between two hearts.

best_reversed.name: Aquamarine
best_reversed.reason: When the union becomes imbalanced, Aquamarine (Water, Communication / Courage) supports the honest conversation that restores the flow.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; the partnership card amplified.

best_daily_wear.name: Rhodochrosite
best_daily_wear.reason: Rhodochrosite worn daily supports self-love as the foundation of equal partnership — only a full cup can meet another full cup.

cards.related_reason: Ace→Two→Three progression in Cups (awakening → union → celebration); Page of Cups is the human apprentice echo; The Lovers (Major 6) shares the mutual-recognition / choice-of-another theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Rose Quartz chosen best_overall per water-suit + heart consensus (Two of Cups = mutual love, Rose Quartz = the partnership stone). Research §3.3 Cups 2 = '妥协 / 吸引 / 和解'.

cards.name: Two of Swords
cards.progression_meaning: stalemate / avoidance / the decision not yet made — the mind holding two opposing truths in suspended tension
cards.progression_position: 2 = the stalemate node of Air's progression: where Two of Wands plans a direction and Two of Cups joins another, Two of Swords cannot do either — the figure blindfolds herself and crosses the blades, refusing to see the choice the Ace's clarity is asking for
cards.upright_meaning: A blindfolded woman sits at the edge of a calm but moonlit sea, two swords crossed horizontally across her chest in a deliberate barrier, the moon waxing behind her and two rocky crags flanking the water. The Two of Swords is the card of suspended stalemate: the mind holding two opposing truths or two painful options in deliberate, defensive balance — choosing neither, seeing neither, keeping the heart and the verdict at arm's length. Upright, this card honours (without shaming) the very human need to pause when the cost of knowing feels too high — but it also names the pause as a choice in itself, one that cannot hold forever.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the blindfold loosens — a decision finally made, information finally received, the crossed swords uncoupling as the truth that was being avoided is allowed to surface. The shadow of the upright was avoidance; the gift of the reversal is the willingness to see. This card invites you to ask what you have been refusing to know, and to consider that the cost of avoiding it has quietly exceeded the cost of facing it.
cards.psychological_lens: decisional conflict + experiential avoidance; the cognitive cost of holding two incompatible beliefs without resolving them
cards.eastern_anchor: The Two of Swords resonates with the Chan Buddhist observation that '知之一字，众妙之门' (the single word 'knowing' is the gate of all wonders) — and the suffering that comes from sealing that gate shut. Selenite, a stone of mental clarity and cleansing across contemplative traditions, pairs with this card's invitation to lift the blindfold and let clarity back in.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a selenite wand; name one truth you have been refusing to look at. Allow yourself to know it for one minute before deciding what to do about it.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Two of Swords signals a relationship at a stalemate — a decision being avoided, a truth withheld, a truce that is really a refusal to see. The card does not predict the outcome; it asks for honesty.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card points to a decision in suspension — two paths, two offers, a difficult call being deferred. The pause has its own cost; information is being withstood.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Two of Swords describes a financial decision avoided — a contract unsigned, a choice deferred, the numbers not yet looked at honestly. Clarity, not more time, is what is missing.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: the avoided truth finally surfaces, the blindfold lifts, the decision gets made — often with relief.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: information received, the stalemate breaks, a clear choice is finally made.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: the avoided financial decision gets faced, the contract read, the numbers examined honestly.

best_overall.name: Selenite
best_overall.reason: Selenite (Air, Clarity / Cleansing) is the blindfold-lifting stone — the clean clarity that gently allows the withheld truth to be seen without forcing it.

best_upright.name: Fluorite
best_upright.reason: Fluorite (Air, Focus / Clarity / Decision) supports honest weighing of the two opposing truths when the mind is ready.

best_reversed.name: Amethyst
best_reversed.reason: When the blindfold lifts and the truth is finally faced, Amethyst (Air, Calm / Spiritual awareness) holds the mind steady through the seeing.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; honesty softened by warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Sodalite
best_daily_wear.reason: Sodalite worn daily supports honest, logical self-inquiry — keeping the gate of knowing open.

cards.related_reason: Ace→Two→Three progression in Swords (clarity → stalemate → heartbreak); Queen of Swords is the mastery that eventually sees through the stalemate; The High Priestess (Major 2) shares the withheld-knowledge theme; The Moon (Major 18) shares the unclear-seeing theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Selenite chosen best_overall per air-suit consensus (Two of Swords = stalemate / blindfold, Selenite = clarity-cleanser). Research §3.3 Swords 2 = '僵局 / 回避 / 脆弱休战'.

cards.name: Three of Swords
cards.progression_meaning: heartbreak / sorrow / the first real friction — the early-expansion node of Air turned painful, where the joining of two truths pierces rather than reconciles
cards.progression_position: 3 = the first-friction node of Air's progression: where the other suits' Three means expansion (Three of Pentacles builds, Three of Cups celebrates), Swords' Three is the famous exception — the sword-pierced heart, the sorrow that follows the stalemate when a truth finally lands
upright_keywords: the piercing of a held hope
cards.upright_meaning: A bright red heart floats against a grey stormy sky, pierced by three swords; behind it heavy rain falls into the same colour as the heart's blood. The Three of Swords is one of the most recognisable images of sorrow in the deck — the moment a truth the heart was holding against breaks through, and the feeling is genuine grief. Upright, this card does not curse or predict disaster; it names a real wound — a loss, a betrayal, a hope pierced by truth — and honours the pain as something that deserves to be felt honestly rather than explained away.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same wound begins to close — healing from grief, the release of long-held pain, forgiveness (of self or other) becoming possible, the worst of the storm already past. The reversal does not erase what happened; it marks the turning point where the heart that broke open can also break free of what was piercing it. This card invites you to ask what grief has been ready to move for some time, and to let the rain wash the wound clean rather than sealing it shut.
cards.psychological_lens: grief processing + emotional disclosure; the empirically documented role of feeling and naming a wound in order to integrate it
cards.eastern_anchor: The Three of Swords resonates with the East Asian image of '破碎虚空' (the breaking open of the apparent world) and the Buddhist practice of sitting with suffering ('苦', dukkha) without fleeing or dressing it up. Rose Quartz, the stone of the heart across both Western and Eastern traditions, pairs with this card's call to hold the wounded heart gently rather than armour it.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a rose-quartz at the heart; name one wound you have been explaining away rather than feeling. Allow yourself one minute of honest grief for it, without needing to fix anything yet.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Three of Swords names real heartbreak — a betrayal, a painful truth surfacing, a relationship wound that needs honest attention. This card is not a curse; it is an invitation to feel what is true and let the wound breathe. (It does not diagnose the relationship as doomed; it asks for honesty.)
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card can signal a painful disappointment — a project rejected, a role lost, a difficult truth about a workplace. Allow the feeling; then assess what the truth is asking you to change.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Three of Swords can name a real financial setback or loss that needs grieving before it can be addressed. This is not investment advice; it acknowledges the feeling and invites clear-eyed attention to the situation.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: healing from heartbreak, forgiveness beginning, the worst of the wound already past, the relationship (or the heart) recovering.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: recovering from a disappointment, the painful truth integrated, a setback becoming a turning point.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: recovering from a financial loss, the setback being addressed honestly. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Rose Quartz
best_overall.reason: Rose Quartz (Heart, unconditional love) is the wounded-heart stone — the gentle holding that lets a real grief be felt and integrated rather than armoured or denied.

best_upright.name: Rhodochrosite
best_upright.reason: Rhodochrosite (Heart, self-love / inner-child healing) is the classic stone for tending a broken heart — the compassion that meets the wound.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When grief is ready to move, Smoky Quartz (grounding + release) gently drains the long-held pain and returns the heart to the body.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; the heart held through its wound.

best_daily_wear.name: Labradorite
best_daily_wear.reason: Labradorite worn daily supports transformation through grief and protects the aura during emotional vulnerability.

cards.related_reason: Two→Three progression in Swords (stalemate → heartbreak); Nine of Swords is the anxiety companion in the shadow triad; The Tower (Major 16) shares the sudden-truth-breaking-through theme; The Star (Major 17) is the healing that follows the wound.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Rose Quartz chosen best_overall per heart-consensus (Three of Swords = heartbreak, Rose Quartz = wounded-heart stone). ⚠️ M3 合规：逆位用 shadow/invitation 口径（healing / releasing / forgiveness），禁 curse/bad omen/disease prediction；yes_no=no 但 reasoning 落该牌独有 heartbreak，非通用 'bad card'。Health 黑名单：不医疗化（不诊断抑郁/焦虑为疾病，不承诺疗效），只描述情绪感受；Finances 黑名单：不投资建议。Research §3.3 Swords 3 = '心碎 / 分离 / 悲伤（著名阴影牌）'.

cards.name: Nine of Swords
cards.progression_meaning: anxiety / the worry that comes at night / the mind turning on itself — the near-completion node of Air, where solitude tips into the rumination of the untended mind
cards.progression_position: 9 = the near-completion / solitude node of Air's progression: where the other suits' Nine means self-sufficiency (Nine of Pentacles) or satisfaction (Nine of Cups), Swords' Nine is the famous shadow — the sleeper sitting upright in bed, nine swords on the wall behind them, the mind that has been left alone too long with its fears
reversed_keywords: the worst imagined rather than real
cards.upright_meaning: A figure sits upright in bed, hands covering their face in despair; nine swords hang in a grid on the wall behind them, and the quilt beneath them is embroidered with roses and the signs of the zodiac. The Nine of Swords is the card of the mind at its most untended — the late-night spiralling, the worry that grows larger in the dark, the way a thought unchecked becomes a catastrophe. Upright, this card does not predict disaster; it names the genuine experience of being caught in anxiety's loop and gently observes that most of what the mind is building in this hour is darker than what is actually true.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same spiral begins to slow — worries releasing, perspective returning, the first light of dawn revealing that the feared catastrophe was largely the mind's own construction, hope becoming possible again. The reversal does not dismiss the worry; it marks the moment the mind is willing to test its fears against reality and reach for support. This card invites you to ask which of your fears has been growing in the dark that daylight and a trusted other's attention would shrink back to its real size.
cards.psychological_lens: rumination + catastrophising + the role of sleep deprivation in amplifying threat perception; the documented gap between feared and actual outcomes
cards.eastern_anchor: The Nine of Swords resonates with the Chan Buddhist observation that '境随心转' (the world follows the turning of the mind) — and the suffering that comes when an untended mind turns against itself in the dark. Amethyst, traditionally the stone of calm and sober seeing across both Western and Eastern traditions, pairs with this card's call to cool the spiralling mind and return it to honest perspective.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an amethyst at the third eye or under the pillow; name one fear that has been growing in the dark. Speak it aloud to a trusted person this week — worry named in daylight rarely keeps the size it had at 3 AM.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Nine of Swords can signal relationship anxiety — the mind spiralling over a partner, a fear that has grown larger in the dark than it deserves. This card does not predict the relationship's failure; it asks which of your worries needs daylight and honest conversation to find its real size.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card points to work-related worry keeping you awake — a fear about a project, a job, a review. Allow the feeling; then test the feared outcome against what you actually know to be true.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Nine of Swords can name financial anxiety — the worry that grows at night. This is not investment advice or a financial forecast; it observes the experience of anxiety and invites clear-eyed attention and, where appropriate, the support of a qualified advisor.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: relationship worry easing, perspective returning, the feared catastrophe revealed as smaller than the mind built it.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: work anxiety easing, the worst imagined rather than real, support arriving.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: financial worry shrinking back to its real size as daylight and clear information arrive. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Amethyst
best_overall.reason: Amethyst (Air, Calm / Spiritual awareness / Protection) is the classic stone for cooling the spiralling mind — the sober, calming quality that returns anxious thought to honest perspective; traditionally placed under the pillow for restful sleep.

best_upright.name: Selenite
best_upright.reason: Selenite (Air, Clarity / Cleansing / Calm) clears the mental loop and brings the clean light of dawn into the darkened room.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When worry is ready to release, Smoky Quartz (grounding + release of fear) gently drains the accumulated dread back into the earth.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; anxiety softened by warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Angelite
best_daily_wear.reason: Angelite worn daily supports calm, gentle communication of fears — bringing the worry into spoken daylight rather than silent spiralling.

cards.name: Ten of Swords
cards.progression_meaning: rock bottom / completion through ending / the cycle hitting its floor — the completion node of Air, where the storm of the suit reaches its absolute limit and the only direction left is dawn
cards.progression_position: 10 = the completion/ending node of Air's progression: where the other suits' Ten means legacy (Ten of Pentacles) or family-harmony (Ten of Cups), Swords' Ten is the famous shadow — the figure lying face-down with ten swords in their back, the storm fully spent, and the dawn breaking on the horizon
cards.upright_meaning: A figure lies face-down on a black-and-grey shore, ten swords driven into their back; the sky is dark with storm but on the far horizon the sun is rising in clear colours between two grey pillars. The Ten of Swords is the most dramatic image in the deck — and also, in practice, one of the kindest. It marks the absolute completion of a cycle, the moment a painful situation has truly hit its floor: there is nothing more the swords can do, because every sword is already in. Upright, this card does not predict death or disaster; it names that an ending has arrived, the worst has already happened, and the only direction left is the dawn that is visibly breaking on the horizon.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same floor begins to lift — recovery, the rising from rock bottom, the worst confirmed as already past, the slow rebuilding after a true ending. The reversal does not undo what ended; it marks the moment the figure is willing to lift their face from the ground and see that the sun has, in fact, been rising the whole time. This card invites you to ask what ending has actually been complete for longer than you have been willing to admit, and what is now waiting on the other side of your willingness to stand up.
cards.psychological_lens: post-traumatic growth + the psychology of 'hitting bottom'; the documented relief that arrives when a feared outcome finally lands and the anticipation ends
cards.eastern_anchor: The Ten of Swords resonates with the East Asian image of '物极必反' (when a thing reaches its extreme, it must turn) — the wisdom that the absolute completion of a cycle contains, by its nature, the seed of its reversal. Smoky Quartz, traditionally a stone of gentle release and grounding, pairs with this card's call to let what is finished be finished and to let the body begin its slow rising.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a smoky-quartz at the root; name one ending that has been complete longer than you have been willing to admit. Allow yourself one breath of grief for it — then one breath of relief that the waiting is over.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Ten of Swords can signal the painful end of a relationship — a true completion, often a betrayal or a long-awaited collapse of what was being held together. This card does not curse the future; it observes that this ending is genuine, the worst has already happened, and the dawn is on the horizon.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card can name the true end of a job, a project, or a professional chapter — often a painful one. Allow the ending to be real; the next chapter cannot begin until this one is allowed to land.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Ten of Swords can mark the floor of a financial difficulty — the moment a setback is fully revealed and the worst is confirmed past. This is not investment advice or a forecast; it observes that clarity has arrived and invites clear-eyed attention to rebuilding.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: recovery from the ending, the worst confirmed past, the slow rebuilding of a heart willing to love again.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: rising from a professional rock bottom, the chapter truly closed, rebuilding beginning.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: the floor reached, recovery beginning, clear-eyed rebuilding. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.reason: Smoky Quartz (grounding + gentle release of what is finished) is the dawn-after-ending stone — the quality that lets a true completion be completed, drains the residual pain, and returns the body to the earth it is ready to rise from.

best_upright.reason: Black Tourmaline (protection / grounding / release of dense energy) holds the field steady during a true ending and shields the body as it processes the collapse.

best_reversed.reason: When the ending begins to lift, Rose Quartz (Heart, unconditional love) gently tends the place where the swords were, and reopens the heart that had to close to survive the cycle.

best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; the heart tended through its ending.

cards.name: Three of Wands
cards.progression_meaning: expansion / first tangible results / looking toward the horizon — the moment Fire's plan becomes a ship already sailed and the figure waits on the height to see what the venture will return
cards.progression_position: 3 = the early-expansion node of Fire's progression: where the Two of Wands only held the globe and planned, the Three of Wands now watches real ships sail on a sea he has committed to — the first forward motion beyond the threshold, the plan becoming a venture already in the world
cards.upright_meaning: A figure in a red robe stands on a high cliff, three wands planted firmly before him; he gazes out over a golden sea where his ships sail toward distant ports — the picture of a venture already in motion, no longer merely planned. Where the Two of Wands held the globe and weighed the direction, the Three of Wands has committed Fire to a course and now watches to see what it will return. Upright, this card is the dignity of the long view — the willingness to invest fire in something whose harvest is still over the horizon, and to trust the work that has already been sent into the world.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same expansion meets headwinds — delays, obstacles, a venture that stalls before its ships come in, or the discovery that the commitment was premature and the horizon farther than it looked. The shadow is not the vision itself; it is the unwillingness to adjust course when reality asks for it. The card invites you to ask which of the ventures you have launched still matches the horizon you actually want, and which needs a course-correction rather than more force.
cards.psychological_lens: long-range planning + delayed-return orientation; the difference between commitment to a venture and stubbornness when the map no longer fits the territory
cards.eastern_anchor: The Three of Wands resonates with the East Asian maritime image of '扬帆远航' (setting sail for distant shores) and the patient confidence of the merchant who has dispatched his cargo and waits on the cliff. Citrine, traditionally a stone of abundance and the merchant's good fortune across both Mediterranean and Asian trade traditions, pairs with this fire-seeded venture already sailing toward its return.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a citrine at the solar plexus; name one venture you have already launched that is still over the horizon. Speak one patient act of tending it this week rather than forcing an early return.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Three of Wands suggests a relationship (or a potential one) whose long-range horizon is being genuinely weighed — the willingness to imagine a future together, not just a present spark. Patient vision, not impulsive leap.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card signals the launched venture — the project, business, or expansion whose first ships are already at sea. Excellent for entrepreneurs, exporters, and anyone whose long-game work is beginning to move.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Three of Wands encourages patient investment in ventures whose returns are not yet visible — and warns against pulling the ship back to shore before it has reached port. Strategic framing, not specific investment advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: impatience with a relationship's pace, premature commitment, or a future-vision that meets friction and stalls.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: expansion delayed, the venture stalled, or committing to a horizon before the map is sound.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: returns delayed, premature over-commitment, the venture needing course-correction. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Citrine
best_overall.reason: Citrine (Fire, Abundance / Motivation / Confidence) is the merchant's expansion stone — the fire-seeded venture sailing toward its return, the patience to wait on the cliff for what was sent out.

cards.name: Four of Wands
cards.progression_meaning: celebration / milestone / a stable moment of arrived joy — the first harvest of Fire's venture, the wands laced into a canopy of welcome
cards.progression_position: 4 = the stabilization/celebration node of Fire's progression: the venture of the Three has reached its first milestone — four wands laced together as a bridal canopy, two figures dancing in the foreground, a castle (homecoming) in the distance
cards.upright_meaning: Four wands are laced together to form a floral canopy; two figures dance beneath them with bunches of myrtle, and a grand castle rises in the distance — the picture of a homecoming, a wedding, a festival, the first stable milestone after a long effort. The Four of Wands is Fire arrived at its first resting place: the venture of the Three has borne its first fruit, and the moment is being honoured. Upright, this card is genuine celebration — a threshold crossed, a milestone reached, the community that gathers to say 'this part is done, and well'.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same celebration meets friction — a delayed milestone, a homecoming that does not quite land, feeling on the outside of the gathering rather than inside it, or the discovery that the threshold was declared crossed before it actually was. The shadow is not the celebration itself; it is the gap between the appearance of arrival and the real foundation beneath it. The card invites you to ask what milestone you have been celebrating that has not actually been crossed yet, and what it would take to make the arrival real.
cards.psychological_lens: savouring + communal ritual; the documented role of marking milestones in consolidating effort and social belonging
cards.eastern_anchor: The Four of Wands resonates with the East Asian festival image of '花门' (the flower gate raised for a wedding or homecoming) and the communal marking of a threshold crossed together. Carnelian, traditionally carried as a stone of joyful vitality and life-force across celebratory traditions, pairs with this Fire arrived at its first festival.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a carnelian; name one milestone you have recently crossed that has not yet been honoured. Speak one act of genuine celebration this week — with at least one other person present.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Four of Wands is one of the most joyful omens — a celebration, an engagement, a wedding, a relationship crossing a recognised threshold together. Real, communal, arrived.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card signals a genuine milestone — a launch completed, a promotion, a project shipped, the team gathering to mark that a stage has truly been crossed.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Four of Wands supports a financial milestone reached and worth honouring — a debt cleared, a savings goal met, a stage of stability worth marking. Not specific financial advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: a delayed celebration, feeling excluded from the gathering, or a threshold declared crossed before the foundation is truly laid.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: a premature milestone, the team not really aligned, the celebration hollow.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: the stability more apparent than real, the milestone not yet fully crossed. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.reason: Carnelian (Fire, Courage / Joy / Vitality) is the celebration stone — the joyful life-force of Fire arrived at its first festival and shared with community.

best_upright.name: Citrine
best_upright.reason: Citrine (Fire, Abundance / Joy) amplifies the abundance of a milestone genuinely reached.

best_reversed.reason: When celebration meets friction or exclusion, Rose Quartz (Heart, warmth) restores the genuine belonging that the gathering was meant to hold.
