cards.name: Eight of Swords
cards.progression_meaning: restriction / feeling trapped / the mind binding itself — Air's restriction node, the blindfolded figure surrounded by eight swords who has not yet noticed the path is open behind her
cards.progression_position: 8 = the mastery/restriction node of Air's progression turned inward: where the Eight of Wands is outward velocity and Eight of Pentacles outward mastery, Swords' Eight is the mind bound — a blindfolded figure stands apparently trapped among eight upright swords, yet her bonds are loose and a castle on open ground waits behind her; the trap is largely the mind's own construction
cards.upright_meaning: A blindfolded woman in a flowing robe stands bound in the open, eight swords arranged upright around her in a loose cage; water laps at her feet and a castle on open ground sits on a hill behind her. She appears trapped — but look again: her bonds are loose, the swords do not form a closed cage, and the path to the castle is open behind her. The Eight of Swords is Air's self-made restriction: the moment the mind has woven a sense of entrapment that is more mental construction than actual cage. Upright, this card does not shame the feeling; it honours how real the trap feels while gently observing that the binds may be looser than they appear.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same restriction begins to release — the blindfold lifting, the path open behind her finally seen, the self-imposed limits recognised as self-imposed, agency returning to the body. The shadow of the upright was the trap of the mind; the gift of the reversal is the willingness to test it against reality. This card invites you to ask which of the 'cages' you have been living inside would, on honest examination, turn out to have an open path behind you that you have not yet let yourself see.
cards.psychological_lens: learned helplessness + cognitive distortion; the documented gap between felt restriction and actual options, and the role of tested agency in dissolving it
cards.eastern_anchor: The Eight of Swords resonates with the Chan Buddhist observation that '作茧自缚' (spinning a cocoon around oneself) is the mind's most reliable suffering, and that the path out is rarely through more thinking. Amethyst, traditionally a stone of calm and sober seeing, pairs with this card's call to lift the blindfold gently and test the cage.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an amethyst at the third eye; name one cage you have been living inside. Ask honestly, without forcing, whether the binds are as tight as they feel — and what one small test of agency would reveal.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Eight of Swords signals feeling trapped in a relationship (or the belief that no relationship is possible) — the sense of restriction that may be more mental construction than actual cage. The card asks for an honest test of whether the path is truly closed.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card points to felt restriction — a role that feels like a trap, options that appear unavailable. The work is to test whether the binds are as tight as the mind has woven.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Eight of Swords can signal a sense of financial entrapment that deserves honest examination — are the options truly closed, or has the mind declared them so? This is strategic framing, not specific investment advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: the blindfold lifting, agency returning, the relationship's real options finally seen.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: the path recognised, the self-imposed limits releasing, the way forward seen.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: the sense of entrapment dissolving as options are honestly examined. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Amethyst
best_overall.reason: Amethyst (Air, Calm / Clarity) is the blindfold-lifting stone — the calm, sober seeing that lets the mind test whether the cage it has woven is actually closed.

best_upright.name: Selenite
best_upright.reason: Selenite (Air, Clarity / Cleansing) clears the mental construction enough to test it against reality.

best_reversed.name: Citrine
best_reversed.reason: When agency returns, Citrine (Fire, Confidence / Motivation) re-activates the will to act on the newly-seen path.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; freedom softened by warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Sodalite
best_daily_wear.reason: Sodalite worn daily supports ongoing honest self-inquiry about which limits are real and which are the mind's own construction.

cards.related_reason: Two→Seven→Eight→Nine progression in Swords (stalemate → strategy → restriction → anxiety); Two of Swords is the earlier blindfold; The Moon (Major 18) shares the unclear-seeing theme; The Devil (Major 15) shares the self-made-bondage theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Amethyst chosen best_overall per air-suit consensus (Eight of Swords = self-made restriction / blindfold, Amethyst = calm-sober-seeing stone). Research §3.3 Swords 8 = '束缚 / 限制 / 困局'.

cards.name: Four of Pentacles
cards.archetype: The Holder of What He Has
cards.progression_meaning: holding / stability / possessiveness — Earth's stabilization node, the figure clutching one pentacle to his chest, one balanced on his head, one under each foot, the desperate grip on what has been gathered
cards.progression_position: 4 = the stabilization/holding node of Earth's progression: where the Three of Pentacles built collaboratively, the Four of Pentacles holds tight — the figure crouching with a coin clutched to his chest, one balanced on his head, one under each foot, security that has curdled into clutching
upright_keywords: the grip on what is gathered
cards.upright_meaning: A figure in a cap and rich robe crouches on a stone bench, one pentacle clutched tight to his chest, another balanced precariously on his head, one under each foot; behind him the city he cannot quite leave. The Four of Pentacles is Earth's holding pattern: the moment the resources of the Ace and the build of the Three have been gathered and are now being clutched — sometimes wisely, sometimes in a grip that has become the cage. Upright, this card honours genuine financial security and the discipline of saving, while gently observing the line where security has crossed into possessiveness that cannot enjoy what it holds.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same grip begins to loosen — generosity returning, control releasing, scarcity loosening its hold, the willingness to hold resources open-handed rather than clutched to the chest. The shadow of the upright was the clutch; the gift of the reversal is the trust that lets abundance flow rather than freeze. This card invites you to ask which of your holdings has become more about fear of loss than enjoyment of what is held, and what open-handed posture the situation is asking for.
cards.psychological_lens: loss aversion + scarcity mindset; the documented tendency to over-weight potential loss relative to gain, and the cost of hoarding to the holder
cards.eastern_anchor: The Four of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian image of '守财奴' (the miser, the cautionary figure whose wealth owns him rather than the reverse) and the Buddhist observation that grasping is itself the suffering. Pyrite, traditionally treasured as a stone of true abundance (rather than 'fool's gold'), pairs with this card's call to distinguish abundance from clutching.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a pyrite stone; name one resource you have been clutching out of fear of loss. Ask whether the grip is protecting the holding or preventing it from being enjoyed or grown.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Four of Pentacles signals possessiveness or holding too tight — jealousy, control, a relationship guarded rather than enjoyed. The card asks whether the grip is protecting the bond or suffocating it.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card points to holding position tightly — security prized over growth, territory guarded, the role that has become a fortification rather than a foundation.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Four of Pentacles can signal healthy saving and security OR — shadow — hoarding that prevents growth. Distinguish stewardship from clutching; this is strategic framing, not specific investment advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: the grip loosening, generosity returning, the relationship re-engaged with open hands.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: releasing the territorial hold, opening to collaboration or growth.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: scarcity loosening, resources flowing, savings moving from frozen hoarding to alive stewardship. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Pyrite
best_overall.reason: Pyrite (Earth, Abundance / Will / Protection) is the true-abundance stone — the quality that distinguishes wealth held wisely from wealth that has come to own the holder; treasured in practice as a stone of real abundance rather than 'fool's gold'.

best_upright.name: Red Jasper
best_upright.reason: Red Jasper (Earth, Grounding / Strength) keeps the holder rooted in the body so security comes from presence rather than clutching.

best_reversed.name: Green Aventurine
best_reversed.reason: When the grip loosens, Aventurine (Opportunity / Growth) re-opens the holder to the flow that clutching had frozen.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; holding softened by warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Jade
best_daily_wear.reason: Jade (Earth, Harmony / Wisdom) worn daily supports the wise, harmonious stewardship that neither hoards nor squanders.

cards.related_reason: Three→Four→Five→Six progression in Pentacles (build → hold → scarcity → give); Six of Pentacles is the balanced-giving companion; The Emperor (Major 4) shares the structured-control theme; The Devil (Major 15) shares the attachment-and-binding theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Pyrite chosen best_overall per earth-suit consensus (Four of Pentacles = holding / possessiveness, Pyrite = true-abundance vs clutching stone). Research §3.3 Pentacles 4 = '占有 / 稳定 / 守财'.

cards.name: Five of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: scarcity / hardship / exclusion — Earth's conflict node, two figures in the snow outside a glowing stained-glass window they cannot enter, five pentacles fixed into the church wall
cards.progression_position: 5 = the conflict/scarcity node of Earth's progression: the holding of the Four has frayed into genuine hardship — two figures (a crippled man and a weary woman) trudge through the snow past a glowing church window whose warmth they cannot reach, five pentacles set into the wall like a consolation they have not yet seen
upright_keywords: the help nearby not yet seen
cards.upright_meaning: Two figures — a man on crutches and a woman wrapped thinly — trudge through falling snow past a glowing stained-glass church window; five pentacles are fixed into the church's wall, lit by the warm light inside that the figures outside cannot reach. The Five of Pentacles is Earth's hardship: the moment material difficulty, exclusion, or illness has left the body in the cold, sometimes unable to see the help that is actually nearby. Upright, this card honours the genuine experience of scarcity without predicting ruin — it names real hardship and gently observes that warm windows exist within reach.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same hardship begins to ease — recovery, help recognised and accepted, the door of the warm building finally seen, the material and spiritual aid that was nearby becoming accessible. The reversal does not erase the cold; it marks the willingness to accept the help and the slow return toward warmth. This card invites you to ask what help you have been passing in the snow that is closer than the hardship allows you to see, and what it would mean to accept it.
cards.psychological_lens: material hardship + perceived social exclusion; the documented tendency of scarcity to narrow attention away from available resources, and the role of accepted help in recovery
cards.eastern_anchor: The Five of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian image of '雪中送炭' (charcoal delivered in the snow) and the Buddhist practice of '布施' (dāna, generous giving) that exists precisely so that no one need stand outside the warm window. Red Jasper, traditionally a stone of endurance through hardship, pairs with this card's call to sustain the body through the cold and notice the help nearby.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a red-jasper at the root; name one hardship you have been trudging through alone. Speak aloud one form of help nearby you have not yet let yourself accept — and one small step toward it this week.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Five of Pentacles signals feeling excluded, alone in the cold, or that a relationship's warmth is no longer reaching you. The card asks what help is closer than the hardship allows you to see, and does not predict the relationship's failure.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card can signal financial hardship, job loss, or feeling excluded from a workplace's warmth. Allow the difficulty; look honestly for the help nearby rather than trudging past it.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Five of Pentacles names genuine financial hardship or a scarcity mindset that narrows attention away from available help. This observes the experience of difficulty and invites money-mindset awareness and acceptance of help; it is not investment advice and does not promise that hardship will end — seek qualified support where appropriate.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: the warmth returning, help accepted, the relationship (or a new connection) offering shelter rather than cold.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: recovery, the helping hand accepted, the workplace's warmth re-engaged.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: hardship easing through accepted help and clearer money-mindset; not a guarantee of wealth, but a return toward workable ground. (Not investment advice — seek qualified counsel for serious financial difficulty.)

best_overall.name: Red Jasper
best_overall.reason: Red Jasper (Earth, Grounding / Strength / Endurance) is the hardship-endurance stone — the steady stamina that sustains the body through the cold until help is recognised and accepted.

best_upright.name: Hematite
best_upright.reason: Hematite (Earth, Grounding / Strength) roots the body in the present so the scarcity does not escalate into panic.

best_reversed.name: Green Aventurine
best_reversed.reason: When recovery begins, Aventurine (Opportunity / Growth) re-opens the eyes to the help and resources nearby.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; the cold softened by warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger's Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger's Eye (Courage / Clarity) worn daily supports the courage to ask for and accept the help that pride or fear has been refusing.

cards.related_reason: Four→Five→Six progression in Pentacles (hold → scarcity → give); Six of Pentacles is the generosity that resolves the scarcity; Nine of Pentacles is the self-sufficiency beyond it; The Star (Major 17) shares the hope-and-renewal theme; The Hermit (Major 9) shares the solitary-in-the-cold theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Red Jasper chosen best_overall per earth-suit consensus (Five of Pentacles = scarcity / hardship / endurance, Red Jasper = hardship-endurance stone). ⚠️ M3 合规：逆位用 recovery / help-accepted 口径（recovery / finding help / door recognised），禁 curse/bad omen/poverty prediction；yes_no=no 但 reasoning 落该牌独有 'hardship + help nearby not yet seen'，非通用 'bad card'——明确写 'does not predict ruin'。Finances 黑名单：不投资建议、不承诺脱贫、明确标注 'not investment advice / seek qualified counsel'，谈 money mindset / 资源觉察 / 接受帮助；Health 黑名单：不医疗化（描述 hardship 为体验，不诊断疾病、不承诺水晶疗效）。Research §3.3 Pentacles 5 = '贫乏 / 困难 / 被排斥'.

cards.name: Nine of Pentacles
cards.progression_meaning: self-sufficiency / abundance earned / the solitary harvest — Earth's near-completion node, the woman in her vineyard with nine pentacles arranged around her and a falcon on her wrist
cards.progression_position: 9 = the near-completion / self-sufficiency node of Earth's progression: the mastery of the Eight has compounded into a settled, single-made abundance — a woman in a beautiful gown stands alone in her vineyard, a hooded falcon on her wrist, nine pentacles arranged like ripe fruit around her, the harvest of disciplined work now enjoyed
cards.upright_meaning: A woman in a beautiful yellow gown stands alone in a lush vineyard; a hooded falcon rests on her gloved wrist, snail at her feet (slow, organic maturation), and nine pentacles are arranged around her in the grapevines like ripe fruit. The Nine of Pentacles is Earth's self-sufficiency: the moment the disciplined mastery of the Eight has compounded into an abundance one person can stand inside and enjoy — alone, not lonely; complete, not waiting. Upright, this card honours the genuine dignity of an earned harvest — the financial, creative, or embodied sufficiency that lets the body rest inside what its own work has grown.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same self-sufficiency either wobbles into over-investment — independence hardened into isolation, the garden enjoyed by no one but the gardener, wealth accumulated without pleasure — or simply pauses, the harvest not yet felt as abundance. The shadow is not the sufficiency itself; it is the refusal of the receive that would let it be enjoyed. This card invites you to ask which part of your earned sufficiency you have been tending without letting yourself taste, and what it would mean to actually live inside the garden you have grown.
cards.psychological_lens: self-concordant achievement + the difference between earned sufficiency and defended self-isolation; the documented role of savouring in converting achievement into wellbeing
cards.eastern_anchor: The Nine of Pentacles resonates with the East Asian ideal of '自给自足' (self-sufficient, providing for oneself) as embodied in the cultivated private garden of the scholar-official — abundance earned through discipline and enjoyed in dignified solitude. Green Aventurine, the stone of opportunity and growth traditionally associated with the garden's abundant yield, pairs with this single-made harvest.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold an aventurine at the heart; name one piece of earned sufficiency you have not yet let yourself enjoy. Speak one act this week of actually living inside the garden you have grown — rather than only tending it.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Nine of Pentacles signals an earned independence that makes partnership a choice rather than a need — the version of you (or a partner) who is already complete and invites another in from fullness, not lack. Often a good omen for mature, healthy relationship.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card marks the moment of earned mastery and self-sufficient success — the business or practice that now runs, the recognition that compounds, the dignified enjoyment of long work.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Nine of Pentacles is one of the most encouraging cards — earned financial self-sufficiency, comfortable independence, abundance grown through discipline. Pair with actual enjoyment, not just accumulation. Not specific investment advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: over-investment in independence, the garden enjoyed alone, or wealth that has not translated into relational warmth.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: success not enjoyed, isolation in plenty, or independence hardened into refusal of collaboration.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: abundance accumulated without pleasure, or false self-sufficiency hiding precarious ground. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Green Aventurine
best_overall.reason: Green Aventurine (Earth, Opportunity / Growth / Abundance) is the self-sufficient-garden stone — the abundant yield of disciplined work, the harvest one can stand inside and enjoy.

best_upright.name: Jade
best_upright.reason: Jade (Earth, Harmony / Wisdom / Good fortune) deepens the dignified, long-horizon enjoyment of the earned garden.

best_reversed.name: Rose Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When sufficiency wobbles into isolation, Rose Quartz (Heart) re-opens the garden to be shared and enjoyed, not only tended.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; fullness shared with warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Pyrite
best_daily_wear.reason: Pyrite (Earth, Abundance / Will) worn daily anchors the will to actually live inside the abundance that has been earned.

cards.related_reason: Eight→Nine→Ten progression in Pentacles (mastery → self-sufficiency → legacy); Ace of Pentacles is the seed that grew into this; The Empress (Major 3) shares the abundant-yield-and-cultivated-garden theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Aventurine chosen best_overall per earth-suit consensus (Nine of Pentacles = self-sufficiency / earned abundance / garden, Aventurine = garden-abundance Earth stone). Research §3.3 Pentacles 9 = '自足 / 丰盛 / 独立'.

cards.name: Ten of Wands
cards.progression_meaning: overload / carrying too much / the completion node of Fire turned heavy — the figure bent under ten wands, approaching the town but unable to set the load down
cards.progression_position: 10 = the completion/overload node of Fire's progression: where the Ten of Pentacles is legacy and Ten of Cups is family-joy, the Ten of Wands is Fire's burden — the figure bent nearly double under a faggot of ten wands, approaching the destination but so loaded that arrival itself has become the problem
reversed_keywords: letting go of what is not yours to carry
cards.upright_meaning: A figure bent nearly double under a heavy bundle of ten wands struggles along a road toward a town in the distance; he is almost there, but the load has grown so heavy that arrival itself is in question, and he has not yet noticed that some of what he carries does not need to make the whole journey. The Ten of Wands is Fire's overload: the moment passion, projects, and commitments — each individually fine — have compounded into a weight the body can barely carry. Upright, this card honours the real experience of responsibility taken on with good intention while gently observing that the carrier has become the load.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same burden begins to release — the load set down, the delegation made, the commitment released that was never really yours, the body straightening as weight lifts. The shadow of the upright was the inability to set it down; the gift of the reversal is the willingness to release. This card invites you to ask which of the wands in your bundle you picked up out of genuine purpose and which out of pride, fear of disappointing, or refusal to ask for help — and which can be set down before the town is reached.
cards.psychological_lens: overcommitment + role overload; the documented cost of accumulated responsibilities and the role of delegation and release in sustainable effort
cards.eastern_anchor: The Ten of Wands resonates with the East Asian image of '放下' (fàngxià, to set down) and the Buddhist observation that the one bent under the load is often the one who chose, wand by wand, to pick each up. Smoky Quartz, traditionally a stone of gentle release and grounding, pairs with this card's call to set down what is not truly yours to carry.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a smoky-quartz at the root; name one wand in your bundle that you picked up out of pride or fear rather than purpose. Permit yourself to set it down — or hand it to someone whose it is — this week.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Ten of Wands signals a relationship in which one partner is carrying too much — or the relationship itself has become a burden of accumulated commitments. The card asks what could be set down or shared rather than heroically dragged to the finish.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card points to overload — too many projects, too many roles, the work that has compounded beyond what one body can carry. Release and delegation are the work, not more effort.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Ten of Wands can signal the burden of too many financial commitments or ventures carried at once. Strategic framing about release and focus, not specific investment advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: the load shared, a commitment released, the relationship lightening as the burden is finally divided.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: delegation made, a project released, the body straightening as the load is shared or set down.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: commitments pruned, focus restored, the load made sustainable. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Smoky Quartz
best_overall.reason: Smoky Quartz (grounding + gentle release of what is finished) is the burden-releasing stone — the quality that lets the carrier set down what is not truly his to carry and return the body to the earth.

best_upright.name: Hematite
best_upright.reason: Hematite (Earth, Grounding / Strength) roots the overloaded body so it does not collapse before the load can be released.

best_reversed.name: Citrine
best_reversed.reason: When the load lifts, Citrine (Fire, Joy / Motivation) re-warms the Fire that overload had flattened back into purpose.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; the burden softened by warmth.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger's Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger's Eye (Clarity / Protection) worn daily supports ongoing discernment of which commitments are truly yours to carry.

cards.related_reason: Eight→Nine→Ten progression in Wands (released → resilience → overload); Ace of Wands is the suit's source; Strength (Major 8) shares the endurance theme; The Tower (Major 16) shares the moment-when-the-load-can-no-longer-be-held theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Smoky Quartz chosen best_overall per earth-grounding consensus (Ten of Wands = burden / overload, Smoky Quartz = gentle-release stone). Research §3.3 Wands 10 = '承载 / 压力 / 传承'.

cards.name: Ten of Cups
cards.suit_theme: emotion / intuition / relationship / creativity of the heart
cards.progression_meaning: family joy / emotional completion / lasting harmony — the completion node of Water, ten cups arranged in a rainbow arc above two embracing figures and their children beneath a joyful sky
cards.progression_position: 10 = the completion/joy node of Water's progression: where Swords' Ten is rock bottom and Pentacles' Ten is legacy-as-wealth, Cups' Ten is the heart's rainbow — ten cups arranged in an arc above a couple and their children dancing beneath, the completion of emotional life into lasting, shared joy
reversed_keywords: the picture not matching the inside
cards.upright_meaning: Ten cups are arranged in a rainbow arc across a bright sky; below, a couple embrace with arms raised while two children dance beside them, and a snug home nestles among trees beside a flowing river. The Ten of Cups is Water's completion: the moment the heart's progression — from Ace's awakening through partnership, friendship, loss, nostalgia, leaving, and fulfilment — arrives at lasting shared joy. Upright, this card is one of the most beloved in the deck: real family warmth, emotional harmony that endures, the home the heart was always reaching toward.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, the same picture meets friction — family disharmony, the values of partners no longer aligned, the idealised image of the home cracking to reveal what was actually underneath, or joy delayed rather than present. The shadow is not the dream of shared joy itself; it is the gap between the picture held up and the truth being lived inside it. The card invites you to ask which idealised image of family or relationship is asking to be reconciled with the reality, and what honest conversation would let the picture and the inside finally match.
cards.psychological_lens: communal flourishing + value congruence; the documented role of shared meaning and aligned values in durable family wellbeing
cards.eastern_anchor: The Ten of Cups resonates with the East Asian ideal of '家和万事兴' (when the family is in harmony, all things flourish) and the rainbow as the classical symbol across both Western and Eastern traditions of the covenant restored after the storm. Rose Quartz, the house stone of the heart across both traditions, pairs with this completion of the heart's rainbow.
cards.recommended_practice: Hold a rose-quartz at the heart; name one relationship or family bond whose harmony you are grateful for. Speak one honest appreciation to someone in that circle this week — and one truth, if any, that the picture has been hiding.
cards.love_meaning: In love readings, the Ten of Cups is one of the most beautiful omens in the deck — lasting partnership, family joy, the relationship that has become home. Real, durable, shared. With the gentle caveat that the picture and the inside must match.
cards.career_meaning: In career, this card signals a workplace (or team) whose values genuinely align with yours — work that feels like belonging, a culture whose harmony is real rather than performed.
cards.finances_meaning: For finances, the Ten of Cups supports heart-aligned financial decisions within family or partnership — shared goals, values-congruent use of resources. Not specific investment advice.
cards.love_meaning_reversed: Reversed in love: family disharmony, values misaligned, the relationship's picture cracking to reveal what needs honest attention.
cards.career_meaning_reversed: Reversed in career: workplace disharmony, values misaligned, the team culture more performed than real.
cards.finances_meaning_reversed: Reversed in finances: shared financial decisions out of alignment, values needing honest re-examination. (Not investment advice.)

best_overall.name: Rose Quartz
best_overall.reason: Rose Quartz (Heart, unconditional love) is the family-joy stone — the heart's completion into lasting, shared harmony; the rainbow of ten cups is the love that endures across a lifetime.

best_upright.name: Moonstone
best_upright.reason: Moonstone (Water, Intuition / New Beginnings / family) deepens the receptive, nurturing quality of the home that has become true.

best_reversed.name: Aquamarine
best_reversed.reason: When the picture meets friction, Aquamarine (Water, Communication / Courage) supports the honest family conversation that realigns values.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Cross-card unified love intention; the heart's rainbow amplified.

best_daily_wear.name: Rhodochrosite
best_daily_wear.reason: Rhodochrosite (Heart, self-love / inner-child healing) worn daily supports the inner-child warmth that lets family joy be received, not just performed.

cards.related_reason: Two→Nine→Ten progression in Cups (union → fulfilment → family); Ace of Cups is the suit's source; The Sun (Major 19) shares the warmth-and-joy theme; The World (Major 21) shares the completion-and-arrival theme.
cards.source_notes: Biddy/Labyrinthos do not list crystals. Rose Quartz chosen best_overall per heart-consensus (Ten of Cups = family joy / emotional completion, Rose Quartz = the family-heart stone). Research §3.3 Cups 10 = '情感和睦 / 家族圆满'.
